The default runtime uses **aiokafka**, but swapping to\nRabbitMQ (via **aio-pika**) or AWS MSK/IAM is trivial — simply implement\n`AbstractBrokerConsumer` and plug it into `EventConsumer`.\n\"\"\"\nfrom __future__ import annotations\n\nimport asyncio\nimport json\nimport logging\nimport os\nimport signal\nimport sys\nimport time\nfrom abc import ABC, abstractmethod\nfrom contextlib import asynccontextmanager\nfrom dataclasses import dataclass\nfrom typing import Any, Awaitable, Callable, Dict, Final, List, Mapping, Type\n\nimport aiokafka\nfrom aiokafka import AIOKafkaConsumer, ConsumerRecord\nfrom pydantic import BaseModel, ValidationError\nfrom tenacity import retry, retry_if_exception_type, stop_after_attempt, wait_exponential\n\n# --------------------------------------------------------------------------- #\n# Configuration & Constants\n# --------------------------------------------------------------------------- #\n\n_LOGGER: Final = logging.getLogger(\"crowdpay.wallet.events.consumer\")\n\nKAFKA_BOOTSTRAP: Final[str] = os.getenv(\"KAFKA_BOOTSTRAP_SERVERS\", \"localhost:9092\")\nWALLET_EVENTS_TOPIC: Final[str] = os.getenv(\"WALLET_EVENTS_TOPIC\", \"wallet.events\")\nSYSTEM_EVENTS_TOPIC: Final[str] = os.getenv(\"SYSTEM_EVENTS_TOPIC\", \"system.events\")\nCONSUMER_GROUP: Final[str] = os.getenv(\"WALLET_CONSUMER_GROUP\", \"wallet_service\")\n\n# --------------------------------------------------------------------------- #\n# Event Model\n# --------------------------------------------------------------------------- #\n\n\nclass Event(BaseModel):\n    \"\"\"\n    Base CloudEvent-ish model.\n\n    All events MUST include at least the following metadata contract:\n    \"\"\"\n\n    id: str\n    source: str\n    type: str\n    spec_version: str = \"1.0\"\n    data: Dict[str, Any]\n    subject: str | None = None\n    time: float | None = None  # Epoch (ms)\n\n\n# Wallet domain-specific events ------------------------------------------------\n\n\nclass WalletCreated(Event):\n    type: str = \"wallet.created\"\n    data: Dict[str, Any]  # { \"wallet_id\": str, \"owner_id\": str, \"currency\": str, ... }\n\n\nclass FundsDeposited(Event):\n    type: str = \"wallet.funds_deposited\"\n    data: Dict[str, Any]  # { \"wallet_id\": str, \"amount\": str, \"currency\": str, ... }\n\n\nclass FundsWithdrawn(Event):\n    type: str = \"wallet.funds_withdrawn\"\n    data: Dict[str, Any]  # { \"wallet_id\": str, \"amount\": str, \"currency\": str, ... }\n\n\nclass TransferInitiated(Event):\n    type: str = \"wallet.transfer_initiated\"\n    data: Dict[str, Any]\n\n\nclass TransferCompleted(Event):\n    type: str = \"wallet.transfer_completed\"\n    data: Dict[str, Any]\n\n\nclass TransferFailed(Event):\n    type: str = \"wallet.transfer_failed\"\n    data: Dict[str, Any]\n\n\n# Register event type mapping for dynamic deserialisation\n_EVENT_REGISTRY: Dict[str, Type[Event]] = {\n    WalletCreated.type: WalletCreated,\n    FundsDeposited.type: FundsDeposited,\n    FundsWithdrawn.type: FundsWithdrawn,\n    TransferInitiated.type: TransferInitiated,\n    TransferCompleted.type: TransferCompleted,\n    TransferFailed.type: TransferFailed,\n}\n\n\n# --------------------------------------------------------------------------- #\n# Domain Handlers (placeholders — would live elsewhere in real code)\n# --------------------------------------------------------------------------- #\n\nasync def handle_wallet_created(event: WalletCreated) -> None:\n    _LOGGER.info(\"🪙 Wallet created ‑ id=%s owner=%s\", event.data[\"wallet_id\"], event.data[\"owner_id\"])\n    # TODO: Persist wallet aggregate / outbox integration event\n    # await wallet_repository.create_wallet(...)\n    # await outbox.publish_async(\"wallet.read_model.updated\", ...)\n\n\nasync def handle_funds_deposited(event: FundsDeposited) -> None:\n    _LOGGER.info(\"💰 Funds deposited ‑ wallet=%s amount=%s%s\",\n                 event.data[\"wallet_id\"], event.data[\"amount\"], event.data[\"currency\"])\n    # TODO: Balance updates / risk checks / ledger postings\n\n\nasync def handle_funds_withdrawn(event: FundsWithdrawn) -> None:\n    _LOGGER.info(\"🏧 Funds withdrawn ‑ wallet=%s amount=%s%s\",\n                 event.data[\"wallet_id\"], event.data[\"amount\"], event.data[\"currency\"])\n    # TODO: Balance validations / AML scanning / ledger postings\n\n\nasync def handle_transfer_initiated(event: TransferInitiated) -> None:\n    _LOGGER.info(\"🔄 Transfer initiated ‑ ref=%s\", event.data.get(\"transfer_ref\"))\n    # TODO: Saga orchestrator handshake\n\n\nasync def handle_transfer_completed(event: TransferCompleted) -> None:\n    _LOGGER.info(\"✅ Transfer completed ‑ ref=%s\", event.data.get(\"transfer_ref\"))\n    # TODO: Finalise ledger / notify parties\n\n\nasync def handle_transfer_failed(event: TransferFailed) -> None:\n    _LOGGER.warning(\"❌ Transfer failed ‑ ref=%s reason=%s\",\n                    event.data.get(\"transfer_ref\"), event.data.get(\"reason\"))\n\n\n# Map event *classes* to concrete async handlers\n_EVENT_HANDLER_MAP: Dict[Type[Event], Callable[[Event], Awaitable[None]]] = {\n    WalletCreated: handle_wallet_created,\n    FundsDeposited: handle_funds_deposited,\n    FundsWithdrawn: handle_funds_withdrawn,\n    TransferInitiated: handle_transfer_initiated,\n    TransferCompleted: handle_transfer_completed,\n    TransferFailed: handle_transfer_failed,\n}\n\n\n# --------------------------------------------------------------------------- #\n# Broker Abstraction\n# --------------------------------------------------------------------------- #\n\nclass AbstractBrokerConsumer(ABC):\n    \"\"\"Strategy interface to allow pluggable broker implementations.\"\"\"\n\n    @abstractmethod\n    async def __aenter__(self) -> \"AbstractBrokerConsumer\":  # noqa: D401\n        ...\n\n    @abstractmethod\n    async def __aexit__(self, exc_type, exc, tb) -> None:  # noqa: D401\n        ...\n\n    @abstractmethod\n    async def start(self) -> None:\n        ...\n\n    @abstractmethod\n    async def stop(self) -> None:\n        ...\n\n    @abstractmethod\n    async def __anext__(self) -> tuple[str, bytes]:\n        \"\"\"Yields (topic, raw_bytes) each iteration.\"\"\"\n        ...\n\n\nclass KafkaConsumerAdapter(AbstractBrokerConsumer):\n    \"\"\"\n    Concrete implementation for Kafka (powered by **aiokafka**).\n\n    Each call to `__anext__` returns the next *raw* (topic, value) message; all\n    offset management is handled internally (manual commit on success).\n    \"\"\"\n\n    def __init__(\n        self,\n        topics: List[str],\n        bootstrap_servers: str,\n        group_id: str,\n        *,\n        enable_auto_commit: bool = False,\n        auto_offset_reset: str = \"earliest\",\n        loop: asyncio.AbstractEventLoop | None = None,\n    ) -> None:\n        self._consumer: AIOKafkaConsumer = AIOKafkaConsumer(\n            *topics,\n            loop=loop,\n            bootstrap_servers=bootstrap_servers,\n            group_id=group_id,\n            enable_auto_commit=enable_auto_commit,\n            auto_offset_reset=auto_offset_reset,\n            value_deserializer=lambda v: v,  # raw bytes -> handled later\n        )\n        self._records: List[ConsumerRecord] = []\n\n    async def __aenter__(self) -> \"KafkaConsumerAdapter\":\n        await self.start()\n        return self\n\n    async def __aexit__(self, exc_type, exc, tb):  # noqa: D401\n        await self.stop()\n\n    async def start(self) -> None:\n        _LOGGER.info(\"Starting Kafka consumer against %s\", KAFKA_BOOTSTRAP)\n        await self._consumer.start()\n\n    async def stop(self) -> None:\n        _LOGGER.info(\"Closing Kafka consumer…\")\n        await self._consumer.stop()\n\n    async def __anext__(self) -> tuple[str, bytes]:\n        \"\"\"\n        Polls until a message is available.  We purposefully block with an\n        infinite timeout to maximise throughput (co-operative cancellation\n        handled by enclosing task).\n        \"\"\"\n        try:\n            record: ConsumerRecord = await self._consumer.getone()\n            self._records.append(record)\n            return record.topic, record.value\n        except asyncio.CancelledError:\n            raise StopAsyncIteration\n        except Exception:\n            _LOGGER.exception(\"Fatal error during Kafka polling loop.\")\n            await asyncio.sleep(1)\n            raise\n\n    async def commit(self) -> None:\n        \"\"\"Commit offsets for all buffered records (if any).\"\"\"\n        if not self._records:\n            return\n        await self._consumer.commit()\n        self._records.clear()\n\n\n# --------------------------------------------------------------------------- #\n# EventConsumer Orchestrator\n# --------------------------------------------------------------------------- #\n\n@dataclass(slots=True)\nclass ProcessingContext:\n    \"\"\"Runtime context used during event processing.\"\"\"\n    topic: str\n    raw_payload: bytes\n    attempts: int = 0\n    received_epoch: float = time.time()\n\n\nclass EventConsumer:\n    \"\"\"\n    Wallet Service top-level event consumer.\n\n    Usage (from `uvicorn` or dedicated entrypoint):\n\n        asyncio.run(EventConsumer().run_forever())\n\n    The **run_forever** coroutine registers a clean shutdown handler (SIGINT /\n    SIGTERM) and blocks the main thread until cancellation.\n    \"\"\"\n\n    def __init__(\n        self,\n        broker_consumer: AbstractBrokerConsumer | None = None,\n        *,\n        loop: asyncio.AbstractEventLoop | None = None,\n    ) -> None:\n        self._loop = loop or asyncio.get_event_loop()\n        self._broker_consumer = broker_consumer or KafkaConsumerAdapter(\n            topics=[WALLET_EVENTS_TOPIC, SYSTEM_EVENTS_TOPIC],\n            bootstrap_servers=KAFKA_BOOTSTRAP,\n            group_id=CONSUMER_GROUP,\n            loop=self._loop,\n        )\n        self._shutdown_event: asyncio.Event = asyncio.Event()\n\n    # --------------------------------------------------------------------- #\n    # Public API\n    # --------------------------------------------------------------------- #\n\n    async def run_forever(self) -> None:\n        self._register_signal_handlers()\n        async with self._broker_consumer:\n            _LOGGER.info(\"WalletService EventConsumer started — awaiting messages…\")\n            while not self._shutdown_event.is_set():\n                try:\n                    topic, raw = await self._broker_consumer.__anext__()\n                    ctx = ProcessingContext(topic=topic, raw_payload=raw)\n                    await self._process_with_retry(ctx)\n                    await self._broker_consumer.commit()\n                except StopAsyncIteration:\n                    break\n                except asyncio.CancelledError:\n                    break\n                except Exception:\n                    _LOGGER.exception(\"Unhandled exception escaped main loop — continuing.\")\n                    # Sleep briefly to avoid tight loop cascade\n                    await asyncio.sleep(1)\n        _LOGGER.info(\"EventConsumer shutdown complete.\")\n\n    # --------------------------------------------------------------------- #\n    # Internals\n    # --------------------------------------------------------------------- #\n\n    @retry(\n        retry=retry_if_exception_type(Exception),\n        wait=wait_exponential(multiplier=1, min=2, max=30),\n        stop=stop_after_attempt(5),\n        reraise=True,\n    )\n    async def _process_with_retry(self, ctx: ProcessingContext) -> None:\n        \"\"\"Process an event with exponential back-off retries.\"\"\"\n        ctx.attempts += 1\n        try:\n            event = self._deserialize(ctx.raw_payload)\n            await self._dispatch(event)\n        except ValidationError as ve:\n            _LOGGER.error(\"Deserialisation error — dropping event (payload=%s) err=%s\",\n                          ctx.raw_payload, ve)\n        except Exception:\n            _LOGGER.exception(\"Processing attempt=%d failed — will retry.\", ctx.attempts)\n            raise\n\n    def _deserialize(self, raw: bytes) -> Event:\n        try:\n            payload: Mapping[str, Any] = json.loads(raw.decode(\"utf8\"))\n        except json.JSONDecodeError as err:\n            _LOGGER.error(\"JSON decode error: %s\", err)\n            raise\n\n        evt_type: str | None = payload.get(\"type\")\n        model_cls = _EVENT_REGISTRY.get(evt_type, Event)  # Fallback to generic\n\n        return model_cls.parse_obj(payload)\n\n    async def _dispatch(self, event: Event) -> None:\n        handler = _EVENT_HANDLER_MAP.get(type(event))\n        if not handler:\n            _LOGGER.debug(\"No handler registered for event type=%s — ignoring.\", event.type)\n            return\n        _LOGGER.debug(\"Dispatching event id=%s type=%s\", event.id, event.type)\n        await handler(event)\n\n    # --------------------------------------------------------------------- #\n    # Graceful shutdown helpers\n    # --------------------------------------------------------------------- #\n\n    def _register_signal_handlers(self) -> None:\n        try:\n            for sig in (signal.SIGINT, signal.SIGTERM):\n                self._loop.add_signal_handler(sig, lambda s=sig: asyncio.create_task(self._shutdown(s)))\n        except NotImplementedError:\n            # Signals are not available on Windows (depending on interpreter)\n            _LOGGER.warning(\"Signal handlers not available — relying on other shutdown hooks.\")\n\n    async def _shutdown(self, sig: signal.Signals) -> None:  # noqa: D401\n        _LOGGER.info(\"Received signal %s — initiating shutdown.\", sig.name)\n        self._shutdown_event.set()\n        # Give consumer some time to flush\n        await asyncio.sleep(0.5)\n        await self._broker_consumer.stop()\n\n\n# --------------------------------------------------------------------------- #\n# Entrypoint (python -m crowdpay_connect.services.wallet_service.app.events.consumer)\n# --------------------------------------------------------------------------- #\n\ndef _configure_logging() -> None:\n    logging.basicConfig(\n        level=logging.INFO,\n        format=\"%(asctime)s %(levelname)-8s %(name)s — %(message)s\",\n        stream=sys.stdout,\n    )\n\n\ndef main() -> None:  # pragma: no cover\n    _configure_logging()\n    _LOGGER.info(\"Bootstrapping WalletService EventConsumer…\")\n    consumer = EventConsumer()\n    try:\n        asyncio.run(consumer.run_forever())\n    except KeyboardInterrupt:\n        pass\n\n\nif __name__ == \"__main__\":  # pragma: no cover\n    main()\n```", "numLines": 416, "startLine": 1, "totalLines": 416}}

1→```python
     2→"""
     3→CrowdPay Connect – Risk & Compliance Service
     4→--------------------------------------------
     5→
     6→core.rule_engine
     7→~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~
     8→
     9→A lightweight, yet production-grade, rule-engine used by the
    10→Risk & Compliance micro-service to perform *real-time* KYC, AML,
    11→and fraud checks on every payment‐related domain event.
    12→
    13→Key design goals
    14→----------------
    15→1. Security-by-Design
    16→2. Hot-swappable rule definitions (YAML/JSON/DB)
    17→3. Deterministic execution & auditability
    18→4. Event-sourcing friendly – every decision is published as an
    19→   immutable ComplianceEvent.
    20→
    21→The engine purposefully avoids *eval()* and executes only a safe
    22→subset of Python AST nodes.  Expressions are compiled into pure
    23→Python callables cached in an LRU for speed.
    24→"""
    25→
    26→from __future__ import annotations
    27→
    28→import ast
    29→import json
    30→import logging
    31→import pathlib
    32→import threading
    33→import types
    34→from dataclasses import dataclass, field
    35→from enum import Enum, auto
    36→from functools import lru_cache, partial
    37→from time import time
    38→from typing import Any, Callable, Dict, Iterable, List, Optional, Sequence
    39→
    40→LOGGER = logging.getLogger("crowdpay.risk_compliance.rule_engine")
    41→LOGGER.addHandler(logging.NullHandler())
    42→
    43→__all__ = [
    44→    "RiskLevel",
    45→    "Decision",
    46→    "Transaction",
    47→    "UserProfile",
    48→    "Rule",
    49→    "RuleResult",
    50→    "RuleEngine",
    51→]
    52→
    53→
    54→# --------------------------------------------------------------------------- #
    55→#  Data-structures                                                            #
    56→# --------------------------------------------------------------------------- #
    57→
    58→
    59→class RiskLevel(Enum):
    60→    """Relative severity of a compliance or fraud finding."""
    61→
    62→    LOW = auto()
    63→    MEDIUM = auto()
    64→    HIGH = auto()
    65→    CRITICAL = auto()
    66→
    67→
    68→class Decision(Enum):
    69→    """Final decision after rule evaluation."""
    70→
    71→    APPROVE = auto()
    72→    MANUAL_REVIEW = auto()
    73→    REJECT = auto()
    74→
    75→
    76→@dataclass(frozen=True, slots=True)
    77→class Transaction:
    78→    """Subset of fields relevant to rule-evaluation.
    79→
    80→    In production, the Transaction object will be hydrated from the event store
    81→    or read side of the CQRS projection layer.
    82→    """
    83→
    84→    tx_id: str
    85→    pod_id: str
    86→    user_id: str
    87→    amount: float
    88→    currency: str
    89→    country: str
    90→    created_at: float
    91→
    92→
    93→@dataclass(frozen=True, slots=True)
    94→class UserProfile:
    95→    """Minimalist representation of a user involved in the transaction."""
    96→
    97→    user_id: str
    98→    kyc_status: str
    99→    reputation_score: float
   100→    country: str
   101→    is_pep: bool  # Politically exposed person
   102→
   103→
   104→@dataclass(slots=True)
   105→class Rule:
   106→    """In-memory representation of a compliance/risk rule."""
   107→
   108→    name: str
   109→    description: str
   110→    priority: int = 50  # Higher priority executed first
   111→    conditions: Sequence[str] = field(default_factory=tuple)
   112→    on_match: Dict[str, Any] = field(default_factory=dict)
   113→    enabled: bool = True
   114→
   115→    # -- runtime compiled artefacts ----------------------------------------- #
   116→    _compiled_conditions: Optional[List[Callable[..., bool]]] = field(
   117→        default=None, init=False, repr=False
   118→    )
   119→
   120→
   121→@dataclass(frozen=True, slots=True)
   122→class RuleResult:
   123→    """Outcome of a single rule evaluation."""
   124→
   125→    rule_name: str
   126→    matched: bool
   127→    risk_level: Optional[RiskLevel] = None
   128→    decision: Optional[Decision] = None
   129→    metadata: Dict[str, Any] = field(default_factory=dict)
   130→
   131→
   132→# --------------------------------------------------------------------------- #
   133→#  Safe expression compiler                                                   #
   134→# --------------------------------------------------------------------------- #
   135→
   136→
   137→_ALLOWED_AST_NODES = {
   138→    ast.Expression,
   139→    ast.BoolOp,
   140→    ast.BinOp,
   141→    ast.UnaryOp,
   142→    ast.IfExp,
   143→    ast.Compare,
   144→    ast.Call,
   145→    ast.Name,
   146→    ast.Load,
   147→    ast.Constant,
   148→    ast.And,
   149→    ast.Or,
   150→    ast.Not,
   151→    ast.Eq,
   152→    ast.NotEq,
   153→    ast.Lt,
   154→    ast.LtE,
   155→    ast.Gt,
   156→    ast.GtE,
   157→    ast.In,
   158→    ast.NotIn,
   159→    ast.Is,
   160→    ast.IsNot,
   161→    ast.Add,
   162→    ast.Sub,
   163→    ast.Mult,
   164→    ast.Div,
   165→    ast.Mod,
   166→    ast.Pow,
   167→    ast.USub,
   168→    ast.Attribute,
   169→}
   170→
   171→
   172→def _safe_compile(expr: str) -> Callable[..., bool]:
   173→    """
   174→    Compile an *expression* into a safe callable.
   175→
   176→    The expression is first validated to contain only non-malicious AST nodes.
   177→    """
   178→    try:
   179→        parsed = ast.parse(expr, mode="eval")
   180→    except SyntaxError as exc:
   181→        raise ValueError(f"Invalid rule condition syntax: {expr!r}") from exc
   182→
   183→    for node in ast.walk(parsed):
   184→        if not isinstance(node, tuple(_ALLOWED_AST_NODES)):
   185→            raise ValueError(
   186→                f"Disallowed operation {node.__class__.__name__} in condition: {expr!r}"
   187→            )
   188→
   189→    compiled = compile(parsed, filename="<rule_condition>", mode="eval")
   190→
   191→    def _predicate(**ctx: Any) -> bool:
   192→        return bool(eval(compiled, {"__builtins__": {}}, ctx))
   193→
   194→    return _predicate
   195→
   196→
   197→@lru_cache(maxsize=512)
   198→def _compile_condition(expr: str) -> Callable[..., bool]:
   199→    """LRU cached wrapper around the AST safe compiler."""
   200→    return _safe_compile(expr)
   201→
   202→
   203→# --------------------------------------------------------------------------- #
   204→#  Compliance event publisher (stub)                                          #
   205→# --------------------------------------------------------------------------- #
   206→
   207→
   208→class ComplianceEventPublisher:
   209→    """Thin wrapper responsible for publishing compliance events.
   210→
   211→    In production this can push to Kafka, NATS Jetstream, or any other event bus.
   212→    """
   213→
   214→    def publish(self, event: Dict[str, Any]) -> None:
   215→        LOGGER.debug("Publishing compliance event: %s", json.dumps(event))
   216→        # Placeholder: plug in your real broker here.
   217→
   218→
   219→# --------------------------------------------------------------------------- #
   220→#  Rule Engine                                                                 #
   221→# --------------------------------------------------------------------------- #
   222→
   223→
   224→class RuleEngine:
   225→    """Evaluates risk & compliance rules on domain events.
   226→
   227→    Thread-safe: rules may be executed concurrently once loaded.
   228→    """
   229→
   230→    _lock: threading.Lock
   231→    _rules: List[Rule]
   232→    _publisher: ComplianceEventPublisher
   233→
   234→    def __init__(
   235→        self,
   236→        rule_sources: Iterable[pathlib.Path | Dict[str, Any]],
   237→        *,
   238→        publisher: Optional[ComplianceEventPublisher] = None,
   239→    ) -> None:
   240→        """
   241→        Parameters
   242→        ----------
   243→        rule_sources:
   244→            Collection of YAML/JSON files *or* pre-parsed rule dictionaries
   245→            from which rules will be loaded at start-up.  Hot reloading can be
   246→            achieved by constructing a new engine instance.
   247→        publisher:
   248→            Event publisher; if ``None``, a default stub is used.
   249→        """
   250→        self._lock = threading.RLock()
   251→        self._rules = []
   252→        self._publisher = publisher or ComplianceEventPublisher()
   253→        self._load_rules(rule_sources)
   254→
   255→    # --------------------------------------------------------------------- #
   256→    #  Public interface                                                     #
   257→    # --------------------------------------------------------------------- #
   258→
   259→    def evaluate(
   260→        self, *, tx: Transaction, user: UserProfile, extra_ctx: Optional[Dict[str, Any]] = None
   261→    ) -> List[RuleResult]:
   262→        """
   263→        Evaluate all active rules against the transaction & user context.
   264→
   265→        Returns a list of RuleResult items sorted by rule priority.
   266→        """
   267→        ctx = {
   268→            "transaction": tx,
   269→            "user": user,
   270→            **(extra_ctx or {}),
   271→        }
   272→        LOGGER.debug("Starting rule evaluation for tx=%s", tx.tx_id)
   273→        results: List[RuleResult] = []
   274→        with self._lock:
   275→            for rule in sorted(self._rules, key=lambda r: r.priority, reverse=True):
   276→                if not rule.enabled:
   277→                    continue
   278→
   279→                if rule._compiled_conditions is None:
   280→                    rule._compiled_conditions = [
   281→                        _compile_condition(expr) for expr in rule.conditions
   282→                    ]
   283→
   284→                matched = all(predicate(**ctx) for predicate in rule._compiled_conditions)
   285→
   286→                if matched:
   287→                    result = self._on_rule_match(rule=rule, ctx=ctx)
   288→                    results.append(result)
   289→                    LOGGER.info(
   290→                        "Rule matched: '%s' (decision=%s, risk=%s) on tx=%s",
   291→                        rule.name,
   292→                        result.decision,
   293→                        result.risk_level,
   294→                        tx.tx_id,
   295→                    )
   296→                else:
   297→                    results.append(
   298→                        RuleResult(
   299→                            rule_name=rule.name,
   300→                            matched=False,
   301→                        )
   302→                    )
   303→
   304→        return results
   305→
   306→    # --------------------------------------------------------------------- #
   307→    #  Internal helpers                                                     #
   308→    # --------------------------------------------------------------------- #
   309→
   310→    def _on_rule_match(self, *, rule: Rule, ctx: Dict[str, Any]) -> RuleResult:
   311→        """
   312→        Build RuleResult, publish event, and return it.
   313→        """
   314→        decision_str = rule.on_match.get("decision", "MANUAL_REVIEW").upper()
   315→        risk_str = rule.on_match.get("risk_level", "MEDIUM").upper()
   316→
   317→        try:
   318→            decision = Decision[decision_str]
   319→        except KeyError:
   320→            decision = Decision.MANUAL_REVIEW
   321→
   322→        try:
   323→            risk = RiskLevel[risk_str]
   324→        except KeyError:
   325→            risk = RiskLevel.MEDIUM
   326→
   327→        result = RuleResult(
   328→            rule_name=rule.name,
   329→            matched=True,
   330→            decision=decision,
   331→            risk_level=risk,
   332→            metadata={k: v for k, v in rule.on_match.items() if k not in {"decision", "risk_level"}},
   333→        )
   334→
   335→        event_payload = {
   336→            "event_id": f"cmpl-{int(time()*1000)}",
   337→            "timestamp": int(time()),
   338→            "rule_name": rule.name,
   339→            "tx_id": ctx["transaction"].tx_id,
   340→            "user_id": ctx["user"].user_id,
   341→            "decision": decision.name,
   342→            "risk_level": risk.name,
   343→            "metadata": result.metadata,
   344→        }
   345→
   346→        self._publisher.publish(event_payload)
   347→        return result
   348→
   349→    def _load_rules(self, sources: Iterable[pathlib.Path | Dict[str, Any]]) -> None:
   350→        """
   351→        Populate in-memory rule list from given sources.
   352→
   353→        Supports JSON (.json) or YAML (.yml/.yaml) files, as well as raw dicts.
   354→        """
   355→        import yaml  # Locally scoped import to avoid dependency if not needed.
   356→
   357→        for src in sources:
   358→            try:
   359→                if isinstance(src, pathlib.Path):
   360→                    if not src.exists():
   361→                        raise FileNotFoundError(src)
   362→                    with src.open("rt", encoding="utf-8") as fp:
   363→                        if src.suffix.lower() in {".yaml", ".yml"}:
   364→                            raw_rules = yaml.safe_load(fp)
   365→                        elif src.suffix.lower() == ".json":
   366→                            raw_rules = json.load(fp)
   367→                        else:
   368→                            LOGGER.warning("Unsupported rule file type: %s", src)
   369→                            continue
   370→                elif isinstance(src, dict):
   371→                    raw_rules = [src]  # Single rule dict
   372→                else:
   373→                    LOGGER.warning("Unknown rule source: %s", src)
   374→                    continue
   375→
   376→                for raw in raw_rules:
   377→                    rule = self._build_rule(raw)
   378→                    self._rules.append(rule)
   379→                    LOGGER.debug("Loaded rule: %s", rule.name)
   380→
   381→            except Exception as exc:  # pylint: disable=broad-except
   382→                LOGGER.error("Failed to load rule from %s: %s", src, exc, exc_info=True)
   383→
   384→        if not self._rules:
   385→            raise RuntimeError("No rules loaded – risk compliance engine cannot start!")
   386→
   387→    @staticmethod
   388→    def _build_rule(data: Dict[str, Any]) -> Rule:
   389→        """Validate and convert raw dict to Rule dataclass."""
   390→        required_fields = {"name", "description", "conditions"}
   391→        missing = required_fields - data.keys()
   392→        if missing:
   393→            raise ValueError(f"Rule definition missing fields: {missing}")
   394→
   395→        return Rule(
   396→            name=data["name"],
   397→            description=data["description"],
   398→            priority=int(data.get("priority", 50)),
   399→            conditions=tuple(map(str.strip, data["conditions"])),
   400→            on_match=dict(data.get("actions") or {}),
   401→            enabled=bool(data.get("enabled", True)),
   402→        )
   403→
   404→
   405→# --------------------------------------------------------------------------- #
   406→#  Example usage (will be stripped during package build)                      #
   407→# --------------------------------------------------------------------------- #
   408→
   409→if __name__ == "__main__":  # pragma: no cover
   410→    logging.basicConfig(level=logging.DEBUG)
   411→
   412→    # Example rule definitions defined inline for demonstration purposes.
   413→    RULES = [
   414→        {
   415→            "name": "HighAmountCrossBorder",
   416→            "description": "Flag cross-border transfers over $10k",
   417→            "priority": 90,
   418→            "conditions": [
   419→                "transaction.amount > 10_000",
   420→                "transaction.country != user.country",
   421→            ],
   422→            "actions": {

   424→                "decision": "MANUAL_REVIEW",

   427→        },
   428→        {
   429→            "name": "UnverifiedUserLargeTx",
   430→            "description": "Reject large transactions from non-KYC verified users",
   431→            "priority": 95,
   432→            "conditions": [
   433→                "user.kyc_status != 'VERIFIED'",
   434→                "transaction.amount >= 5_000",
   435→            ],
   436→            "actions": {

   438→                "decision": "REJECT",
   439→                "alert_code": "KYC-UVU-002",
   440→            },
   441→        },
   442→    ]
   443→
   444→    engine = RuleEngine(rule_sources=[RULES[0], RULES[1]])
   445→
   446→    result = engine.evaluate(
   447→        tx=Transaction(
   448→            tx_id="TX123456",
   449→            pod_id="POD987",
   450→            user_id="USER42",
   451→            amount=12_500,
   452→            currency="USD",
   453→            country="US",
   454→            created_at=time(),
   455→        ),
   456→        user=UserProfile(

   459→            reputation_score=42.0,
   460→            country="CA",
   461→            is_pep=False,
   462→        ),
   463→    )
   464→
   465→    for r in result:

     2→"""
     3→crowdpay_connect.services.risk_compliance_service.app.api.v1.assessment
     4→
     5→FastAPI router exposing version 1 endpoints for the Risk & Compliance Service.
     6→This module is responsible for orchestrating real-time risk assessments that
     7→determine whether a payment, transfer, or CrowdPod operation may proceed.
     8→
     9→Although in a full production deployment most collaborators (DB repository,
    10→message bus, risk-scoring engine) are imported from dedicated packages,
    11→light-weight fallbacks are provided here so the module remains functional and
    12→executable in isolation (e.g., for documentation builds or CI static analysis).
    13→"""
    14→from __future__ import annotations
    15→
    16→import enum
    17→import logging
    18→import uuid
    19→from datetime import datetime, timezone
    20→from typing import Dict, Optional
    21→
    22→from fastapi import APIRouter, BackgroundTasks, Depends, HTTPException, status
    23→from pydantic import BaseModel, Field, PositiveFloat, constr, validator
    24→
    25→logger = logging.getLogger(__name__)
    26→
    27→# --------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
    28→# Domain / Shared kernel
    29→# --------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
    30→
    31→
    32→class AssessmentStatus(str, enum.Enum):
    33→    """Life-cycle states that a risk assessment can be in."""
    34→
    35→    PENDING = "pending"
    36→    COMPLETED = "completed"
    37→    FAILED = "failed"
    38→
    39→
    40→class AssessmentDecision(str, enum.Enum):
    41→    """Possible decisions after running the Risk Engine."""
    42→
    43→    APPROVED = "approved"
    44→    HOLD = "hold"
    45→    DECLINED = "declined"
    46→
    47→
    48→class RiskAssessment(BaseModel):
    49→    """Aggregate root for a risk assessment."""
    50→
    51→    assessment_id: uuid.UUID
    52→    request_ts: datetime
    53→    completed_ts: Optional[datetime]
    54→    status: AssessmentStatus
    55→    decision: Optional[AssessmentDecision]
    56→    score: Optional[float] = Field(
    57→        None,
    58→        ge=0.0,
    59→        le=1.0,
    60→        description="Normalized risk score in range 0.0-1.0 where 1.0 is maximum risk",
    61→    )
    62→    reference_id: str = Field(
    63→        ...,
    64→        description=(
    65→            "External reference – e.g., payment intent id or CrowdPod id – used when "
    66→            "publishing events so other services can correlate results."
    67→        ),
    68→    )
    69→
    70→
    71→# --------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
    72→# DTOs
    73→# --------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
    74→
    75→
    76→class AssessmentIn(BaseModel):
    77→    """
    78→    Payload accepted by POST /assessment.
    79→
    80→    currency_code: ISO-4217 alpha-3
    81→    """
    82→
    83→    reference_id: constr(strip_whitespace=True, min_length=8, max_length=64)
    84→    user_id: constr(strip_whitespace=True, min_length=8, max_length=64)

    91→    @validator("currency_code")

    96→class AssessmentOut(BaseModel):
    97→    assessment_id: uuid.UUID
    98→    status: AssessmentStatus
    99→    decision: Optional[AssessmentDecision]
   100→    score: Optional[float]
   101→    request_ts: datetime
   102→    completed_ts: Optional[datetime]
   103→    reference_id: str
   104→
   105→
   106→# --------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
   107→# Infrastructure placeholders (repository, message bus, risk engine)
   108→# --------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
   109→
   110→
   111→class _InMemoryAssessmentRepository:
   112→    """
   113→    Simplistic repository implementation that keeps all assessments in memory.
   114→
   115→    Production deployments are expected to inject a persistence-backed repository
   116→    (e.g., Postgres or DynamoDB implementation).

   118→
   119→    _store: Dict[uuid.UUID, RiskAssessment] = {}
   120→
   121→    def add(self, assessment: RiskAssessment) -> None:
   122→        logger.debug("Persisting assessment %s", assessment.assessment_id)
   123→        self._store[assessment.assessment_id] = assessment
   124→
   125→    def get(self, assessment_id: uuid.UUID) -> Optional[RiskAssessment]:
   126→        return self._store.get(assessment_id)
   127→
   128→    def update(self, assessment: RiskAssessment) -> None:
   129→        logger.debug("Updating assessment %s", assessment.assessment_id)
   130→        self._store[assessment.assessment_id] = assessment
   131→
   132→
   133→class _NoOpMessageBus:
   134→    """
   135→    No-op message bus that simply logs published events.
   136→
   137→    Swap in a real publisher (e.g., Kafka, NATS, SNS) via dependency injection.
   138→    """
   139→
   140→    def publish(self, topic: str, payload: dict) -> None:
   141→        logger.info("Publishing event %s with payload=%s", topic, payload)
   142→
   143→
   144→class _RiskEngine:
   145→    """
   146→    Very small, naïve risk scoring stub.
   147→
   148→    Real implementations would incorporate:
   149→      * Deterministic features – KYC tier, country, device fingerprint
   150→      * Behavioural analytics – velocity checks, reputational signals
   151→      * Graph heuristics – CrowdPod network trust, follower endorsements
   152→      * Machine-learning ensembles
   153→    """
   154→
   155→    HIGH_RISK_CURRENCIES = {"NGN", "KHR", "LAK"}  # Example high-risk fiat
   156→
   157→    def score(
   158→        self,
   159→        amount: float,
   160→        currency_code: str,
   161→        user_reputation: float,
   162→        pod_reputation: float,
   163→    ) -> float:
   164→        """
   165→        Returns a float in the range [0, 1] representing risk probability.
   166→        """
   167→        base = min(1.0, amount / 10_000)  # scale amount to 0-1
   168→        currency_modifier = 0.15 if currency_code in self.HIGH_RISK_CURRENCIES else 0
   169→        reputation_modifier = (1 - user_reputation) * 0.4 + (1 - pod_reputation) * 0.2
   170→        score = min(1.0, base + currency_modifier + reputation_modifier)
   171→        logger.debug(
   172→            "Calculated risk score %.4f (base=%.4f currency_mod=%.4f rep_mod=%.4f)",
   173→            score,
   174→            base,
   175→            currency_modifier,
   176→            reputation_modifier,
   177→        )
   178→        return score
   179→
   180→    def decide(self, score: float) -> AssessmentDecision:
   181→        if score < 0.5:
   182→            return AssessmentDecision.APPROVED
   183→        if score < 0.75:
   184→            return AssessmentDecision.HOLD
   185→        return AssessmentDecision.DECLINED

   187→
   188→# Global singleton fallbacks – these would be wired using FastAPI dependencies in real
   189→# codebase, but initialised here for brevity.
   190→_repository = _InMemoryAssessmentRepository()
   191→_bus = _NoOpMessageBus()
   192→_engine = _RiskEngine()

   198→router = APIRouter(prefix="/api/v1/assessment", tags=["Risk & Compliance"])

   201→def get_repository() -> _InMemoryAssessmentRepository:

   203→
   204→
   205→def get_bus() -> _NoOpMessageBus:

   212→

   214→# Routes
   215→# --------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

   217→
   218→@router.post(
   219→    "",
   220→    response_model=AssessmentOut,

   222→    summary="Trigger a new risk assessment",

   224→def create_assessment(
   225→    payload: AssessmentIn,
   226→    background_tasks: BackgroundTasks,
   227→    repo: _InMemoryAssessmentRepository = Depends(get_repository),
   228→    bus: _NoOpMessageBus = Depends(get_bus),
   229→    engine: _RiskEngine = Depends(get_risk_engine),
   230→) -> AssessmentOut:
   231→    """
   232→    Accepts an assessment request, persists it as *pending*, triggers asynchronous
   233→    evaluation in a background task, and returns immediately. Consumers can poll
   234→    `GET /assessment/{assessment_id}` for final decision.
   235→    """

   239→        request_ts=datetime.now(tz=timezone.utc),

   241→        completed_ts=None,
   242→        decision=None,
   243→        score=None,
   244→        reference_id=payload.reference_id,
   245→    )
   246→    repo.add(assessment)

   248→    background_tasks.add_task(
   249→        _run_risk_assessment_task,
   250→        assessment=assessment,
   251→        payload=payload,
   252→        repo=repo,
   253→        bus=bus,
   254→        engine=engine,
   255→    )
   256→
   257→    return AssessmentOut.parse_obj(assessment.dict())
   258→
   259→
   260→@router.get(
   261→    "/{assessment_id}",
   262→    response_model=AssessmentOut,
   263→    summary="Fetch the latest risk assessment state",
   264→)
   265→def read_assessment(
   266→    assessment_id: uuid.UUID,
   267→    repo: _InMemoryAssessmentRepository = Depends(get_repository),
   268→) -> AssessmentOut:
   269→    assessment = repo.get(assessment_id)
   270→    if not assessment:
   271→        raise HTTPException(
   272→            status_code=status.HTTP_404_NOT_FOUND,
   273→            detail="Assessment not found",
   274→        )
   275→    return AssessmentOut.parse_obj(assessment.dict())
   276→
   277→
   278→# --------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
   279→# Background job
   280→# --------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
   281→
   282→
   283→def _run_risk_assessment_task(
   284→    assessment: RiskAssessment,
   285→    payload: AssessmentIn,
   286→    repo: _InMemoryAssessmentRepository,
   287→    bus: _NoOpMessageBus,
   288→    engine: _RiskEngine,
   289→) -> None:
   290→    """
   291→    Performs potentially expensive / IO heavy risk scoring logic. Executed by
   292→    FastAPI's `BackgroundTasks`, Celery worker, or any async task execution pool.
   293→    """
   294→    logger.info("Starting risk evaluation for %s", assessment.assessment_id)
   295→
   296→    try:
   297→        # ------------------------------------------------------------
   298→        # Enrich features – in production these would be remote calls
   299→        # ------------------------------------------------------------
   300→        user_reputation = _fetch_user_reputation(payload.user_id)
   301→        pod_reputation = _fetch_pod_reputation(payload.pod_id)
   302→
   303→        # ------------------------------------------------------------
   304→        # Core risk scoring
   305→        # ------------------------------------------------------------
   306→        score = engine.score(
   307→            amount=float(payload.amount),
   308→            currency_code=payload.currency_code,
   309→            user_reputation=user_reputation,
   310→            pod_reputation=pod_reputation,
   311→        )
   312→        decision = engine.decide(score)
   313→
   314→        # ------------------------------------------------------------
   315→        # Persist outcome
   316→        # ------------------------------------------------------------
   317→        assessment.completed_ts = datetime.now(tz=timezone.utc)
   318→        assessment.status = AssessmentStatus.COMPLETED
   319→        assessment.decision = decision
   320→        assessment.score = score
   321→        repo.update(assessment)
   322→
   323→        # ------------------------------------------------------------
   324→        # Notify interested subscribers
   325→        # ------------------------------------------------------------
   326→        bus.publish(
   327→            topic="risk.assessment.completed",
   328→            payload={
   329→                "assessment_id": str(assessment.assessment_id),
   330→                "reference_id": assessment.reference_id,
   331→                "decision": assessment.decision,
   332→                "score": assessment.score,
   333→                "ts": assessment.completed_ts.isoformat(),
   334→            },
   335→        )
   336→
   337→        logger.info(
   338→            "Risk evaluation completed for %s (decision=%s score=%.4f)",
   339→            assessment.assessment_id,
   340→            decision,
   341→            score,
   342→        )
   343→
   344→    except Exception as exc:  # pylint: disable=broad-except
   345→        logger.exception(
   346→            "Risk evaluation failed for %s due to an unexpected error: %s",
   347→            assessment.assessment_id,
   348→            exc,
   349→        )
   350→        assessment.completed_ts = datetime.now(tz=timezone.utc)
   351→        assessment.status = AssessmentStatus.FAILED
   352→        repo.update(assessment)
   353→        bus.publish(
   354→            topic="risk.assessment.failed",
   355→            payload={
   356→                "assessment_id": str(assessment.assessment_id),
   357→                "reference_id": assessment.reference_id,
   358→                "error": str(exc),
   359→                "ts": assessment.completed_ts.isoformat(),
   360→            },
   361→        )
   362→
   363→
   364→# --------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
   365→# Lightweight mocks for enrichment look-ups
   366→# --------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
   367→
   368→
   369→def _fetch_user_reputation(user_id: str) -> float:
   370→    """
   371→    Placeholder for a call into the 'Profile / Social Graph' micro-service that
   372→    exposes a reputation API. Returns 1.0 for maximum trust.
   373→    """
   374→    logger.debug("Fetching reputation for user %s", user_id)
   375→    # The following heuristic is purely illustrative.
   376→    return 0.9 if int(user_id[-1], 36) % 2 == 0 else 0.7
   377→
   378→
   379→def _fetch_pod_reputation(pod_id: str) -> float:
   380→    """
   381→    Placeholder for CrowdPod trust score (derived from up-votes, audit logs, etc.)
   382→    """
   383→    logger.debug("Fetching reputation for pod %s", pod_id)
   384→    return 0.95 if int(pod_id[-1], 36) % 3 == 0 else 0.6

   127→###############################################################################
   128→# Exceptions
   129→###############################################################################
   130→
   131→
   132→class SagaError(RuntimeError):
   133→    """Base class for saga repository exceptions."""
   134→
   135→
   136→class SagaNotFoundError(SagaError):
   137→    """Saga instance could not be found."""
   138→
   139→
   140→class SagaVersionConflictError(SagaError):
   141→    """
   142→    Saga concurrent modification error—optimistic locking version mismatch.
   143→    """
   144→
   145→
   146→###############################################################################
   147→# Repository
   148→###############################################################################
   149→
   150→
   151→class SagaRepository:
   152→    """
   153→    Repository providing CRUD operations for Saga instances.
   154→    Intended to be injected as a dependency from FastAPI/DI container.
   155→    """
   156→
   157→    def __init__(self, session: AsyncSession):
   158→        self._session = session
   159→
   160→    # --------------------------------------------------------------------- #
   161→    # Saga operations
   162→    # --------------------------------------------------------------------- #
   163→
   164→    async def create_saga(
   165→        self,
   166→        *,
   167→        reference_id: str,
   168→        saga_type: str,
   169→        steps: Sequence[str],
   170→        metadata: Optional[Dict[str, Any]] = None,
   171→    ) -> SagaORM:
   172→        """
   173→        Create a new Saga instance and its initial steps.
   174→
   175→        :param reference_id: External reference for idempotency (e.g., tx id).
   176→        :param saga_type: Name of the Saga orchestrator (e.g., SETTLEMENT_SAGA).
   177→        :param steps: Ordered collection of step names.
   178→        :param metadata: Optional arbitrary JSON metadata.
   179→        """
   180→        logger.debug("Creating saga (ref=%s, type=%s)", reference_id, saga_type)
   181→        saga = SagaORM(reference_id=reference_id, saga_type=saga_type, metadata=metadata)
   182→        saga.steps = [
   183→            SagaStepORM(name=name, order=idx, status=SagaStepStatus.PENDING) for idx, name in enumerate(steps)
   184→        ]
   185→        self._session.add(saga)
   186→
   187→        try:
   188→            await self._session.commit()
   189→        except IntegrityError as exc:
   190→            await self._session.rollback()
   191→            logger.error("Failed to create saga %s - %s", reference_id, exc)
   192→            raise SagaError("Saga creation failed") from exc
   193→
   194→        logger.info("Saga created [%s]", saga.id)
   195→        return saga
   196→
   197→    async def get_saga(self, saga_id: uuid.UUID, *, for_update: bool = False) -> SagaORM:
   198→        """
   199→        Fetch Saga by ID.
   200→
   201→        :param saga_id: Saga UUID.
   202→        :param for_update: Whether to place a SELECT ... FOR UPDATE lock.
   203→        :raises SagaNotFoundError: If saga is missing.
   204→        """
   205→        stmt = select(SagaORM).where(SagaORM.id == saga_id)
   206→        if for_update:
   207→            stmt = stmt.with_for_update()
   208→
   209→        logger.debug("Fetching saga %s (lock=%s)", saga_id, for_update)
   210→        result = await self._session.execute(stmt)
   211→        saga = result.scalar_one_or_none()
   212→
   213→        if saga is None:
   214→            raise SagaNotFoundError(f"Saga {saga_id} not found")
   215→
   216→        return saga
   217→
   218→    async def update_saga_status(
   219→        self,
   220→        saga_id: uuid.UUID,
   221→        *,
   222→        status: SagaStatus,
   223→        expected_version: Optional[int] = None,
   224→    ) -> SagaORM:
   225→        """
   226→        Atomically update Saga status (optimistic locking via version column).
   227→
   228→        :param saga_id: Saga UUID.
   229→        :param status: New status.
   230→        :param expected_version: If supplied, will ensure version match.
   231→        :raises SagaVersionConflictError: If optimistic lock fails.
   232→        """
   233→        logger.debug(
   234→            "Updating saga %s status to %s (expected_version=%s)",
   235→            saga_id,
   236→            status,
   237→            expected_version,
   238→        )
   239→
   240→        stmt = (
   241→            update(SagaORM)
   242→            .where(SagaORM.id == saga_id)
   243→            .values(
   244→                status=status,
   245→                updated_at=dt.datetime.utcnow(),
   246→                version=SagaORM.version + 1,
   247→            )
   248→            .returning(SagaORM)
   249→        )
   250→
   251→        if expected_version is not None:
   252→            stmt = stmt.where(SagaORM.version == expected_version)
   253→
   254→        result = await self._session.execute(stmt)
   255→        saga: SagaORM | None = result.scalar_one_or_none()
   256→
   257→        if saga is None:
   258→            await self._session.rollback()
   259→            raise SagaVersionConflictError(
   260→                f"Optimistic lock failed for saga {saga_id} "
   261→                f"(expected_version={expected_version})"
   262→            )
   263→
   264→        await self._session.commit()
   265→        logger.info("Saga %s status updated to %s", saga_id, status)
   266→        return saga
   267→
   268→    # --------------------------------------------------------------------- #
   269→    # Step operations
   270→    # --------------------------------------------------------------------- #
   271→
   272→    async def mark_step_completed(
   273→        self,
   274→        saga_id: uuid.UUID,
   275→        step_name: str,
   276→        *,
   277→        output: Optional[Dict[str, Any]] = None,
   278→    ) -> SagaStepORM:
   279→        """
   280→        Update the step's status to COMPLETED.
   281→        """
   282→        logger.debug("Marking step '%s' completed for saga %s", step_name, saga_id)
   283→        stmt = (
   284→            update(SagaStepORM)
   285→            .where(
   286→                SagaStepORM.saga_id == saga_id,
   287→                SagaStepORM.name == step_name,
   288→                SagaStepORM.status == SagaStepStatus.PENDING,
   289→            )
   290→            .values(
   291→                status=SagaStepStatus.COMPLETED,
   292→                output=output,
   293→                updated_at=dt.datetime.utcnow(),
   294→            )
   295→            .returning(SagaStepORM)
   296→        )
   297→        result = await self._session.execute(stmt)
   298→        step: SagaStepORM | None = result.scalar_one_or_none()
   299→
   300→        if step is None:
   301→            await self._session.rollback()
   302→            logger.warning("Step '%s' not found or already processed (saga=%s)", step_name, saga_id)
   303→            raise SagaNotFoundError(f"Step {step_name} not found for saga {saga_id}")
   304→
   305→        await self._session.commit()
   306→        logger.info("Step '%s' completed (saga=%s)", step_name, saga_id)
   307→        return step
   308→
   309→    async def mark_step_failed(
   310→        self,
   311→        saga_id: uuid.UUID,
   312→        step_name: str,
   313→        *,
   314→        reason: str,
   315→        output: Optional[Dict[str, Any]] = None,
   316→    ) -> SagaStepORM:
   317→        """
   318→        Mark the given step as FAILED.
   319→        """
   320→        logger.debug("Marking step '%s' failed for saga %s: %s", step_name, saga_id, reason)
   321→        stmt = (
   322→            update(SagaStepORM)
   323→            .where(
   324→                SagaStepORM.saga_id == saga_id,
   325→                SagaStepORM.name == step_name,
   326→                SagaStepORM.status.in_(
   327→                    [SagaStepStatus.PENDING, SagaStepStatus.COMPLETED]
   328→                ),  # allow update unless already failed
   329→            )
   330→            .values(
   331→                status=SagaStepStatus.FAILED,
   332→                output={**(output or {}), "reason": reason},
   333→                updated_at=dt.datetime.utcnow(),
   334→            )
   335→            .returning(SagaStepORM)
   336→        )
   337→        result = await self._session.execute(stmt)
   338→        step: SagaStepORM | None = result.scalar_one_or_none()
   339→
   340→        if step is None:
   341→            await self._session.rollback()
   342→            raise SagaNotFoundError(f"Step {step_name} not found for saga {saga_id}")
   343→
   344→        await self._session.commit()
   345→        logger.error("Step '%s' FAILED (saga=%s): %s", step_name, saga_id, reason)
   346→        return step
   347→
   348→    async def list_pending_steps(self, saga_id: uuid.UUID) -> List[SagaStepORM]:
   349→        """
   350→        Return steps that are not completed/compensated/failed yet.
   351→        """
   352→        stmt = select(SagaStepORM).where(
   353→            SagaStepORM.saga_id == saga_id,
   354→            SagaStepORM.status == SagaStepStatus.PENDING,
   355→        ).order_by(SagaStepORM.order)
   356→
   357→        result = await self._session.execute(stmt)
   358→        steps = result.scalars().all()
   359→        return list(steps)
   360→
   361→    # --------------------------------------------------------------------- #
   362→    # Convenience helpers
   363→    # --------------------------------------------------------------------- #
   364→
   365→    async def compensate_saga(self, saga_id: uuid.UUID, *, reason: str) -> None:
   366→        """
   367→        Mark all COMPLETED steps in reverse order for compensation, then set
   368→        saga status to COMPENSATED.
   369→
   370→        This operation is executed transactionally.
   371→        """
   372→        logger.warning("Compensating saga %s due to: %s", saga_id, reason)
   373→        saga = await self.get_saga(saga_id, for_update=True)
   374→
   375→        # 1. Mark steps
   376→        for step in reversed(saga.steps):
   377→            if step.status == SagaStepStatus.COMPLETED:
   378→                logger.debug("Compensating step '%s' (saga=%s)", step.name, saga_id)
   379→                step.status = SagaStepStatus.COMPENSATED
   380→                step.output = {**(step.output or {}), "compensation_reason": reason}
   381→
   382→        saga.status = SagaStatus.COMPENSATED
   383→
   384→        await self._session.commit()
   385→        logger.info("Saga %s compensated", saga_id)
   386→
   387→    async def delete_old_sagas(self, *, older_than_days: int = 30, limit: int = 500) -> int:
   388→        """
   389→        House-keeping utility. Remove sagas that ended > X days ago.
   390→
   391→        Returns number of rows deleted.
   392→        """
   393→        dead_line = dt.datetime.utcnow() - dt.timedelta(days=older_than_days)
   394→        logger.debug("Pruning sagas completed before %s", dead_line.isoformat())
   395→
   396→        stmt_select = select(SagaORM.id).where(
   397→            SagaORM.updated_at < dead_line,
   398→            SagaORM.status.in_([SagaStatus.COMPLETED, SagaStatus.COMPENSATED, SagaStatus.FAILED]),
   399→        ).limit(limit)
   400→
   401→        result = await self._session.execute(stmt_select)
   402→        ids = [row.id for row in result.fetchall()]
   403→
   404→        if not ids:
   405→            return 0
   406→
   407→        logger.info("Deleting %d old sagas", len(ids))
   408→        await self._session.execute(
   409→            update(SagaStepORM).where(SagaStepORM.saga_id.in_(ids)).execution_options(synchronize_session=False)
   410→        )
   411→        await self._session.execute(
   412→            update(SagaORM).where(SagaORM.id.in_(ids)).execution_options(synchronize_session=False)
   413→        )
   414→        await self._session.commit()
   415→        return len(ids)
   416→
   417→    # --------------------------------------------------------------------- #
   418→    # Utilities
   419→    # --------------------------------------------------------------------- #
   420→
   421→    async def refresh(self, saga: SagaORM) -> None:
   422→        """
   423→        Ensure the given Saga ORM instance is fresh from DB.
   424→        """
   425→        await self._session.refresh(saga)
   426→
   427→    async def close(self) -> None:
   428→        """
   429→        Close underlying session gracefully.
   430→        """
   431→        if self._session and not self._session.closed:
   432→            await self._session.close()
   433→
   434→###############################################################################
   435→# Async context manager sugar
   436→###############################################################################
   437→
   438→
   439→class _SagaRepositoryContext:
   440→    """
   441→    Convenience async context manager for short-lived repository use:
   442→
   443→    async with saga_repository(session_factory) as repo:
   444→        ...
   445→    """
   446→
   447→    def __init__(self, session_factory):
   448→        self._session_factory = session_factory
   449→        self._repo: Optional[SagaRepository] = None
   450→
   451→    async def __aenter__(self) -> SagaRepository:
   452→        session: AsyncSession = await self._session_factory()
   453→        self._repo = SagaRepository(session)
   454→        return self._repo
   455→
   456→    async def __aexit__(self, exc_type, exc, tb):
   457→        if self._repo:
   458→            if exc:
   459→                await self._repo._session.rollback()
   460→            await self._repo.close()
   461→
   462→
   463→def saga_repository(session_factory) -> _SagaRepositoryContext:
   464→    """
   465→    Factory returning an async context manager.
   466→
   467→    Usage:
   468→        async with saga_repository(session_factory) as repo:
   469→            await repo.create_saga(...)
   470→    """
   471→    return _SagaRepositoryContext(session_factory)
   472→```

raise SagaError(\"Saga creation failed\") from exc\n\n        logger.info(\"Saga created [%s]\", saga.id)\n        return saga\n\n    async def get_saga(self, saga_id: uuid.UUID, *, for_update: bool = False) -> SagaORM:\n        \"\"\"\n        Fetch Saga by ID.\n\n        :param saga_id: Saga UUID.\n        :param for_update: Whether to place a SELECT ... FOR UPDATE lock.\n        :raises SagaNotFoundError: If saga is missing.\n        \"\"\"\n        stmt = select(SagaORM).where(SagaORM.id == saga_id)\n        if for_update:\n            stmt = stmt.with_for_update()\n\n        logger.debug(\"Fetching saga %s (lock=%s)\", saga_id, for_update)\n        result = await self._session.execute(stmt)\n        saga = result.scalar_one_or_none()\n\n        if saga is None:\n            raise SagaNotFoundError(f\"Saga {saga_id} not found\")\n\n        return saga\n\n    async def update_saga_status(\n        self,\n        saga_id: uuid.UUID,\n        *,\n        status: SagaStatus,\n        expected_version: Optional[int] = None,\n    ) -> SagaORM:\n        \"\"\"\n        Atomically update Saga status (optimistic locking via version column).\n\n        :param saga_id: Saga UUID.\n        :param status: New status.\n        :param expected_version: If supplied, will ensure version match.\n        :raises SagaVersionConflictError: If optimistic lock fails.\n        \"\"\"\n        logger.debug(\n            \"Updating saga %s status to %s (expected_version=%s)\",\n            saga_id,\n            status,\n            expected_version,\n        )\n\n        stmt = (\n            update(SagaORM)\n            .where(SagaORM.id == saga_id)\n            .values(\n                status=status,\n                updated_at=dt.datetime.utcnow(),\n                version=SagaORM.version + 1,\n            )\n            .returning(SagaORM)\n        )\n\n        if expected_version is not None:\n            stmt = stmt.where(SagaORM.version == expected_version)\n\n        result = await self._session.execute(stmt)\n        saga: SagaORM | None = result.scalar_one_or_none()\n\n        if saga is None:\n            await self._session.rollback()\n            raise SagaVersionConflictError(\n                f\"Optimistic lock failed for saga {saga_id} \"\n                f\"(expected_version={expected_version})\"\n            )\n\n        await self._session.commit()\n        logger.info(\"Saga %s status updated to %s\", saga_id, status)\n        return saga\n\n    # --------------------------------------------------------------------- #\n    # Step operations\n    # --------------------------------------------------------------------- #\n\n    async def mark_step_completed(\n        self,\n        saga_id: uuid.UUID,\n        step_name: str,\n        *,\n        output: Optional[Dict[str, Any]] = None,\n    ) -> SagaStepORM:\n        \"\"\"\n        Update the step's status to COMPLETED.\n        \"\"\"\n        logger.debug(\"Marking step '%s' completed for saga %s\", step_name, saga_id)\n        stmt = (\n            update(SagaStepORM)\n            .where(\n                SagaStepORM.saga_id == saga_id,\n                SagaStepORM.name == step_name,\n                SagaStepORM.status == SagaStepStatus.PENDING,\n            )\n            .values(\n                status=SagaStepStatus.COMPLETED,\n                output=output,\n                updated_at=dt.datetime.utcnow(),\n            )\n            .returning(SagaStepORM)\n        )\n        result = await self._session.execute(stmt)\n        step: SagaStepORM | None = result.scalar_one_or_none()\n\n        if step is None:\n            await self._session.rollback()\n            logger.warning(\"Step '%s' not found or already processed (saga=%s)\", step_name, saga_id)\n            raise SagaNotFoundError(f\"Step {step_name} not found for saga {saga_id}\")\n\n        await self._session.commit()\n        logger.info(\"Step '%s' completed (saga=%s)\", step_name, saga_id)\n        return step\n\n    async def mark_step_failed(\n        self,\n        saga_id: uuid.UUID,\n        step_name: str,\n        *,\n        reason: str,\n        output: Optional[Dict[str, Any]] = None,\n    ) -> SagaStepORM:\n        \"\"\"\n        Mark the given step as FAILED.\n        \"\"\"\n        logger.debug(\"Marking step '%s' failed for saga %s: %s\", step_name, saga_id, reason)\n        stmt = (\n            update(SagaStepORM)\n            .where(\n                SagaStepORM.saga_id == saga_id,\n                SagaStepORM.name == step_name,\n                SagaStepORM.status.in_(\n                    [SagaStepStatus.PENDING, SagaStepStatus.COMPLETED]\n                ),  # allow update unless already failed\n            )\n            .values(\n                status=SagaStepStatus.FAILED,\n                output={**(output or {}), \"reason\": reason},\n                updated_at=dt.datetime.utcnow(),\n            )\n            .returning(SagaStepORM)\n        )\n        result = await self._session.execute(stmt)\n        step: SagaStepORM | None = result.scalar_one_or_none()\n\n        if step is None:\n            await self._session.rollback()\n            raise SagaNotFoundError(f\"Step {step_name} not found for saga {saga_id}\")\n\n        await self._session.commit()\n        logger.error(\"Step '%s' FAILED (saga=%s): %s\", step_name, saga_id, reason)\n        return step\n\n    async def list_pending_steps(self, saga_id: uuid.UUID) -> List[SagaStepORM]:\n        \"\"\"\n        Return steps that are not completed/compensated/failed yet.\n        \"\"\"\n        stmt = select(SagaStepORM).where(\n            SagaStepORM.saga_id == saga_id,\n            SagaStepORM.status == SagaStepStatus.PENDING,\n        ).order_by(SagaStepORM.order)\n\n        result = await self._session.execute(stmt)\n        steps = result.scalars().all()\n        return list(steps)\n\n    # --------------------------------------------------------------------- #\n    # Convenience helpers\n    # --------------------------------------------------------------------- #\n\n    async def compensate_saga(self, saga_id: uuid.UUID, *, reason: str) -> None:\n        \"\"\"\n        Mark all COMPLETED steps in reverse order for compensation, then set\n        saga status to COMPENSATED.\n\n        This operation is executed transactionally.\n        \"\"\"\n        logger.warning(\"Compensating saga %s due to: %s\", saga_id, reason)\n        saga = await self.get_saga(saga_id, for_update=True)\n\n        # 1. Mark steps\n        for step in reversed(saga.steps):\n            if step.status == SagaStepStatus.COMPLETED:\n                logger.debug(\"Compensating step '%s' (saga=%s)\", step.name, saga_id)\n                step.status = SagaStepStatus.COMPENSATED\n                step.output = {**(step.output or {}), \"compensation_reason\": reason}\n\n        saga.status = SagaStatus.COMPENSATED\n\n        await self._session.commit()\n        logger.info(\"Saga %s compensated\", saga_id)\n\n    async def delete_old_sagas(self, *, older_than_days: int = 30, limit: int = 500) -> int:\n        \"\"\"\n        House-keeping utility. Remove sagas that ended > X days ago.\n\n        Returns number of rows deleted.\n        \"\"\"\n        dead_line = dt.datetime.utcnow() - dt.timedelta(days=older_than_days)\n        logger.debug(\"Pruning sagas completed before %s\", dead_line.isoformat())\n\n        stmt_select = select(SagaORM.id).where(\n            SagaORM.updated_at < dead_line,\n            SagaORM.status.in_([SagaStatus.COMPLETED, SagaStatus.COMPENSATED, SagaStatus.FAILED]),\n        ).limit(limit)\n\n        result = await self._session.execute(stmt_select)\n        ids = [row.id for row in result.fetchall()]\n\n        if not ids:\n            return 0\n\n        logger.info(\"Deleting %d old sagas\", len(ids))\n        await self._session.execute(\n            update(SagaStepORM).where(SagaStepORM.saga_id.in_(ids)).execution_options(synchronize_session=False)\n        )\n        await self._session.execute(\n            update(SagaORM).where(SagaORM.id.in_(ids)).execution_options(synchronize_session=False)\n        )\n        await self._session.commit()\n        return len(ids)\n\n    # --------------------------------------------------------------------- #\n    # Utilities\n    # --------------------------------------------------------------------- #\n\n    async def refresh(self, saga: SagaORM) -> None:\n        \"\"\"\n        Ensure the given Saga ORM instance is fresh from DB.\n        \"\"\"\n        await self._session.refresh(saga)\n\n    async def close(self) -> None:\n        \"\"\"\n        Close underlying session gracefully.\n        \"\"\"\n        if self._session and not self._session.closed:\n            await self._session.close()\n\n###############################################################################\n# Async context manager sugar\n###############################################################################\n\n\nclass _SagaRepositoryContext:\n    \"\"\"\n    Convenience async context manager for short-lived repository use:\n\n    async with saga_repository(session_factory) as repo:\n        ...\n    \"\"\"\n\n    def __init__(self, session_factory):\n        self._session_factory = session_factory\n        self._repo: Optional[SagaRepository] = None\n\n    async def __aenter__(self) -> SagaRepository:\n        session: AsyncSession = await self._session_factory()\n        self._repo = SagaRepository(session)\n        return self._repo\n\n    async def __aexit__(self, exc_type, exc, tb):\n        if self._repo:\n            if exc:\n                await self._repo._session.rollback()\n            await self._repo.close()\n\n\ndef saga_repository(session_factory) -> _SagaRepositoryContext:\n    \"\"\"\n    Factory returning an async context manager.\n\n    Usage:\n        async with saga_repository(session_factory) as repo:\n            await repo.create_saga(...)\n    \"\"\"\n    return _SagaRepositoryContext(session_factory)\n```", "numLines": 472, "startLine": 1, "totalLines": 472}}

1→```markdown 2→# CrowdPay Connect • Transaction Service 3→ 4→Transactional micro–service responsible for **orchestrating, persisting and 5→settling every monetary movement** inside a CrowdPod. 6→Implements **CQRS + Event-Sourcing**, participates in **distributed Sagas** and 7→enforces **Security-by-Design** principles (KYC, fraud-detection, 8→rate-limiting, audit-trail). 9→ 10→--- 11→ 12→## 1. Responsibilities 13→ 14→1. **Command Side** – accepts _Transfer_, _Top-Up_, _Withdrawal_ or _FX Swap_ 15→ commands, validates them against: 16→ • risk‐engine, • KYC state, • account limits, • FX quotation TTL. 17→2. **Event Store** – append-only stream (`postgresql` → `wal2json` logical 18→ slot) storing immutable `TransactionEvent`s. 19→3. **Query Side** – pre-computed projections kept in `read_model.transactions` 20→ for low-latency reads / reporting. 21→4. **Saga Coordination** – publishes domain events to the message broker 22→ (`NATS JetStream`) and listens for compensation commands in case of failure 23→ (e.g. failed FX settlement, AML hit). 24→5. **Compliance Trail** – ships signed hash of every event to the `audit-vault` 25→ micro-service for tamper-proof archiving. 26→ 27→--- 28→ 29→## 2. Service APIs 30→ 31→| Method | Transport | Auth | Description | 32→|----------------------------------|-----------|-------------|------------------------------------------| 33→| POST /v1/transactions | REST | JWT + mTLS | Initiate a new transaction command | 34→| GET /v1/transactions/{id} | REST | JWT + mTLS | Fetch aggregate / projections | 35→| gRPC `TransactionService.Create` | gRPC | mutual TLS | Low-latency ingestion for internal calls | 36→ 37→> NOTE 38→> Public SDKs wrap these endpoints and transparently handle retries, 39→> idempotency keys, and saga correlation IDs. 40→ 41→--- 42→ 43→## 3. Quick-Start (local) 44→ 45→```bash 46→# 1. Clone mono-repo 47→git clone git@github.com:crowdpay/connect.git 48→cd connect/services/transaction_service 49→ 50→# 2. Bootstrap Docker stack 51→make infra-up # postgres, nats, jaeger, redis, audit-vault 52→make migrate # executes Alembic migrations 53→make seed-secrets # generates self-signed certs for mTLS 54→make dev # reloads on code changes 55→``` 56→ 57→Environment variables (see `.env.example`): 58→ 59→``` 60→DATABASE_URL=postgresql+asyncpg://user:pwd@localhost:5432/transactions 61→MESSAGE_BROKER_URL=nats://localhost:4222 62→JWT_PUBLIC_KEY_PATH=/secrets/jwks.json 63→SENTRY_DSN= 64→FX_QUOTE_SERVICE_URL=http://fx:8000 65→``` 66→ 67→--- 68→ 69→## 4. Folder Structure 70→ 71→``` 72→transaction_service/ 73→├── adapters/ # IO / Framework glue code 74→│ ├── http/ # FastAPI routers & DTOs 75→│ ├── grpc/ # gRPC generated stubs 76→│ └── messaging/ # NATS JetStream publisher / consumer 77→├── application/ # CQRS command & query handlers 78→├── domain/ # Pure business logic, aggregates & value objects 79→├── infra/ # DB models, migrations, observability, cache 80→└── tests/ # pytest with Hypothesis property-based tests 81→``` 82→ 83→--- 84→ 85→## 5. Code Samples 86→ 87→### 5.1 Creating a Transaction (Python SDK) 88→ 89→```python 90→from crowdpay_connect.sdk import CrowdPayClient 91→from crowdpay_connect.sdk.models import ( 92→ Money, 93→ TransactionCommand, 94→ TransactionType, 95→) 96→ 97→client = CrowdPayClient( 98→ base_url="https://api.crowdpay.io", 99→ api_key="cp_live_xxx", # automatically exchanged for JWT 100→ tls_cert="/path/to/client-cert.pem", 101→ tls_key="/path/to/client-key.pem", 102→) 103→ 104→cmd = TransactionCommand( 105→ pod_id="pod_abcd1234", 106→ type=TransactionType.TRANSFER, 107→ amount=Money(currency="EUR", value="250.00"), 108→ beneficiary_wallet_id="wallet_xyz", 109→ description="Weekend trip booking", 110→ idempotency_key="b54e26a1-90fc-4dc6-95b2-7c9f3d2f24ab", 111→) 112→ 113→tx = client.transactions.create(cmd) 114→print(tx.status) # -> PENDING 115→``` 116→ 117→### 5.2 Subscribing to Events (internal service) 118→ 119→```python 120→import asyncio 121→from crowdpay_connect.event_bus import EventBus 122→from crowdpay_connect.events import TransactionSettled, TransactionFailed 123→ 124→async def handle_settled(evt: TransactionSettled) -> None: 125→ print(f"[✓] Transaction {evt.transaction_id} settled in ledger.") 126→ 127→async def handle_failed(evt: TransactionFailed) -> None: 128→ print(f"[✗] Transaction {evt.transaction_id} rolled back: {evt.reason}") 129→ 130→bus = EventBus("nats://nats:4222") 131→ 132→bus.subscribe(TransactionSettled, handle_settled) 133→bus.subscribe(TransactionFailed, handle_failed) 134→ 135→asyncio.run(bus.run_forever()) 136→``` 137→ 138→### 5.3 Compensation Workflow (Saga) 139→ 140→```python 141→from transaction_service.application.commands import CompensateFxLeg 142→from transaction_service.domain.saga import saga_manager 143→ 144→async def compensate_failed_fx(tx_id: str, reason: str) -> None: 145→ cmd = CompensateFxLeg(transaction_id=tx_id, failure_reason=reason) 146→ await saga_manager.handle(cmd) 147→``` 148→ 149→--- 150→ 151→## 6. Error Codes 152→ 153→| Code | HTTP | Meaning | 154→|------|------|----------------------------------------------------------------| 155→| T001 | 422 | Validation failed (limits, KYC, sanctions) | 156→| T002 | 409 | Duplicate idempotency key | 157→| T003 | 503 | Upstream dependency unavailable (FX, Risk Engine, Ledger) | 158→| T004 | 402 | Insufficient funds after pre-authorization | 159→| T005 | 500 | Unexpected server error – error reference broadcast to Sentry | 160→ 161→All errors embed a `trace_id` header for correlation across micro-services. 162→ 163→--- 164→ 165→## 7. Testing & Quality Gates 166→ 167→• **Unit / Integration tests** → `pytest -q` (95 %+ coverage enforced) 168→• **Static Analysis** → `ruff` + `mypy --strict` + `bandit -r .` 169→• **Load tests** → `k6` scripts in `infra/load/` targeting 1 k TPS, <50 ms p95 170→• **Chaos / Saga fault-injection** → `litmuschaos` experiments: randomly abort 171→ FX legs and verify compensation. 172→ 173→CI pipeline hosted on **GitHub Actions**; all gates must pass before merge. 174→ 175→--- 176→ 177→## 8. Observability 178→ 179→Metric / Trace | Tool | Example 180→--------------------------|--------|-------------------------------------------- 181→Latency (ms), TPS | Prometheus + Grafana | `transaction_processing_latency_p95` 182→Distributed Traces | Jaeger | view saga spans across services 183→Audit Trail Verification | Audit-Vault | merkle root per 10 000 events 184→Error Percent / SLO | Sentry | alert at 1 % 5-min error rate 185→ 186→--- 187→ 188→## 9. Security Considerations 189→ 190→• mTLS mutual authentication between micro-services 191→• **OPA** side-cars evaluate ABAC policies on impersonation and circle roles 192→• Event payloads **field-level encrypted** for PII (`ChaCha20-Poly1305`) 193→• Periodic **CST** (Continuous Security Testing) scan → `trivy` & `semgrep` 194→• One-time secrets loaded via **HashiCorp Vault** with short-lived leases 195→ 196→--- 197→ 198→## 10. Roadmap 199→ 200→1. Add lattice-based **confidential computing** enclave for AML model execution 201→2. Integrate **ISO-20022** messaging for bank settlements 202→3. Roll-out **WebAuthn** transactional signing (FIDO2) for high-value flows 203→ 204→--- 205→ 206→
207→ 208→Made with 💜 by the CrowdPay Connect Core Payments Team 209→_“Trust by Design, at Social Scale.”_ 210→ 211→
