[metadata] {"type": "text", "file": {"filePath": "/app/project/services/transaction_service/README.md", "content": "```markdown\n# CrowdPay Connect • Transaction Service\n\nTransactional micro–service responsible for **orchestrating, persisting and\nsettling every monetary movement** inside a CrowdPod. \nImplements **CQRS + Event-Sourcing**, participates in **distributed Sagas** and\nenforces **Security-by-Design** principles (KYC, fraud-detection,\nrate-limiting, audit-trail).\n\n---\n\n## 1. Responsibilities\n\n1. **Command Side** – accepts _Transfer_, _Top-Up_, _Withdrawal_ or _FX Swap_\n commands, validates them against:\n • risk‐engine, • KYC state, • account limits, • FX quotation TTL.\n2. **Event Store** – append-only stream (`postgresql` → `wal2json` logical\n slot) storing immutable `TransactionEvent`s.\n3. **Query Side** – pre-computed projections kept in `read_model.transactions`\n for low-latency reads / reporting.\n4. **Saga Coordination** – publishes domain events to the message broker\n (`NATS JetStream`) and listens for compensation commands in case of failure\n (e.g. failed FX settlement, AML hit).\n5. **Compliance Trail** – ships signed hash of every event to the `audit-vault`\n micro-service for tamper-proof archiving.\n\n---\n\n## 2. Service APIs\n\n| Method | Transport | Auth | Description |\n|----------------------------------|-----------|-------------|------------------------------------------|\n| POST /v1/transactions | REST | JWT + mTLS | Initiate a new transaction command |\n| GET /v1/transactions/{id} | REST | JWT + mTLS | Fetch aggregate / projections |\n| gRPC `TransactionService.Create` | gRPC | mutual TLS | Low-latency ingestion for internal calls |\n\n> NOTE \n> Public SDKs wrap these endpoints and transparently handle retries,\n> idempotency keys, and saga correlation IDs.\n\n---\n\n## 3. Quick-Start (local)\n\n```bash\n# 1. Clone mono-repo\ngit clone git@github.com:crowdpay/connect.git\ncd connect/services/transaction_service\n\n# 2. Bootstrap Docker stack\nmake infra-up # postgres, nats, jaeger, redis, audit-vault\nmake migrate # executes Alembic migrations\nmake seed-secrets # generates self-signed certs for mTLS\nmake dev # reloads on code changes\n```\n\nEnvironment variables (see `.env.example`):\n\n```\nDATABASE_URL=postgresql+asyncpg://user:pwd@localhost:5432/transactions\nMESSAGE_BROKER_URL=nats://localhost:4222\nJWT_PUBLIC_KEY_PATH=/secrets/jwks.json\nSENTRY_DSN=\nFX_QUOTE_SERVICE_URL=http://fx:8000\n```\n\n---\n\n## 4. Folder Structure\n\n```\ntransaction_service/\n├── adapters/ # IO / Framework glue code\n│ ├── http/ # FastAPI routers & DTOs\n│ ├── grpc/ # gRPC generated stubs\n│ └── messaging/ # NATS JetStream publisher / consumer\n├── application/ # CQRS command & query handlers\n├── domain/ # Pure business logic, aggregates & value objects\n├── infra/ # DB models, migrations, observability, cache\n└── tests/ # pytest with Hypothesis property-based tests\n```\n\n---\n\n## 5. Code Samples\n\n### 5.1 Creating a Transaction (Python SDK)\n\n```python\nfrom crowdpay_connect.sdk import CrowdPayClient\nfrom crowdpay_connect.sdk.models import (\n Money,\n TransactionCommand,\n TransactionType,\n)\n\nclient = CrowdPayClient(\n base_url=\"https://api.crowdpay.io\",\n api_key=\"cp_live_xxx\", # automatically exchanged for JWT\n tls_cert=\"/path/to/client-cert.pem\",\n tls_key=\"/path/to/client-key.pem\",\n)\n\ncmd = TransactionCommand(\n pod_id=\"pod_abcd1234\",\n type=TransactionType.TRANSFER,\n amount=Money(currency=\"EUR\", value=\"250.00\"),\n beneficiary_wallet_id=\"wallet_xyz\",\n description=\"Weekend trip booking\",\n idempotency_key=\"b54e26a1-90fc-4dc6-95b2-7c9f3d2f24ab\",\n)\n\ntx = client.transactions.create(cmd)\nprint(tx.status) # -> PENDING\n```\n\n### 5.2 Subscribing to Events (internal service)\n\n```python\nimport asyncio\nfrom crowdpay_connect.event_bus import EventBus\nfrom crowdpay_connect.events import TransactionSettled, TransactionFailed\n\nasync def handle_settled(evt: TransactionSettled) -> None:\n print(f\"[✓] Transaction {evt.transaction_id} settled in ledger.\")\n\nasync def handle_failed(evt: TransactionFailed) -> None:\n print(f\"[✗] Transaction {evt.transaction_id} rolled back: {evt.reason}\")\n\nbus = EventBus(\"nats://nats:4222\")\n\nbus.subscribe(TransactionSettled, handle_settled)\nbus.subscribe(TransactionFailed, handle_failed)\n\nasyncio.run(bus.run_forever())\n```\n\n### 5.3 Compensation Workflow (Saga)\n\n```python\nfrom transaction_service.application.commands import CompensateFxLeg\nfrom transaction_service.domain.saga import saga_manager\n\nasync def compensate_failed_fx(tx_id: str, reason: str) -> None:\n cmd = CompensateFxLeg(transaction_id=tx_id, failure_reason=reason)\n await saga_manager.handle(cmd)\n```\n\n---\n\n## 6. Error Codes\n\n| Code | HTTP | Meaning |\n|------|------|----------------------------------------------------------------|\n| T001 | 422 | Validation failed (limits, KYC, sanctions) |\n| T002 | 409 | Duplicate idempotency key |\n| T003 | 503 | Upstream dependency unavailable (FX, Risk Engine, Ledger) |\n| T004 | 402 | Insufficient funds after pre-authorization |\n| T005 | 500 | Unexpected server error – error reference broadcast to Sentry |\n\nAll errors embed a `trace_id` header for correlation across micro-services.\n\n---\n\n## 7. Testing & Quality Gates\n\n• **Unit / Integration tests** → `pytest -q` (95 %+ coverage enforced) \n• **Static Analysis** → `ruff` + `mypy --strict` + `bandit -r .` \n• **Load tests** → `k6` scripts in `infra/load/` targeting 1 k TPS, <50 ms p95 \n• **Chaos / Saga fault-injection** → `litmuschaos` experiments: randomly abort\n FX legs and verify compensation.\n\nCI pipeline hosted on **GitHub Actions**; all gates must pass before merge.\n\n---\n\n## 8. Observability\n\nMetric / Trace | Tool | Example\n--------------------------|--------|--------------------------------------------\nLatency (ms), TPS | Prometheus + Grafana | `transaction_processing_latency_p95`\nDistributed Traces | Jaeger | view saga spans across services\nAudit Trail Verification | Audit-Vault | merkle root per 10 000 events\nError Percent / SLO | Sentry | alert at 1 % 5-min error rate\n\n---\n\n## 9. Security Considerations\n\n• mTLS mutual authentication between micro-services \n• **OPA** side-cars evaluate ABAC policies on impersonation and circle roles \n• Event payloads **field-level encrypted** for PII (`ChaCha20-Poly1305`) \n• Periodic **CST** (Continuous Security Testing) scan → `trivy` & `semgrep` \n• One-time secrets loaded via **HashiCorp Vault** with short-lived leases\n\n---\n\n## 10. Roadmap\n\n1. Add lattice-based **confidential computing** enclave for AML model execution \n2. Integrate **ISO-20022** messaging for bank settlements \n3. Roll-out **WebAuthn** transactional signing (FIDO2) for high-value flows\n\n---\n\n
\n\nMade with 💜 by the CrowdPay Connect Core Payments Team \n_“Trust by Design, at Social Scale.”_\n\n
\n```", "numLines": 212, "startLine": 1, "totalLines": 212}}

# INSTANT_SETTLEMENT_PROPOSAL.md

## Architectural Proposal: "Instant Settlements" Feature for CrowdPay Connect

**Author:** Lead Architect
**Version:** 1.0
**Date:** 2026-02-02
**Status:** Draft - For Review

---

## Executive Summary

This document proposes an architectural approach to integrate the "Instant Settlement" premium feature into CrowdPay Connect, enabling select users to have their transactions settled in near real-time through integration with a new third-party provider, **InstaPay**. The proposal addresses how to incorporate this synchronous settlement path while preserving the platform's existing architectural principles: event-driven design, Saga-based transaction orchestration, and event-sourced ledger integrity.

---

## 1. Analysis of the Current Architecture

### 1.1 Standard Payment Settlement Flow

The current CrowdPay Connect platform processes payments through a **distributed Saga pattern** with multiple asynchronous steps designed for resilience and standard settlement timelines (T+2 days). The flow is orchestrated by the `transaction_service` and involves several bounded contexts:

**Key Services Involved:**
1. **Transaction Service** - Orchestrates the payment Saga, manages saga state, publishes domain events
2. **Wallet Service** - Manages wallet balances via an immutable, append-only double-entry ledger
3. **Risk & Compliance Service** - Performs real-time KYC, AML, and fraud checks
4. **KYC Service** - Verifies customer identity
5. **FX Service** - Handles currency conversion for multi-currency payments

**Current Payment Saga Steps** (as defined in `services/transaction_service/app/sagas/payment_saga.py:238-273`):
```
1. KYC Verification      → Verify payer has valid KYC profile
2. Risk Assessment       → Real-time transaction risk evaluation
3. Debit Payer          → Reserve/debit funds from payer's wallet
4. Currency Conversion   → Convert if source/target currencies differ
5. Credit CrowdPod      → Credit funds to destination wallet
6. Settlement           → Mark transaction as settled (T+2 timeline)
```

Each step has an associated **compensation action** that executes in reverse order if any step fails, ensuring eventual consistency (see `payment_saga.py:275-306`).

### 1.2 Saga Pattern Implementation

The platform uses an **orchestration-based Saga pattern** (as documented in `docs/architecture/saga_pattern.md`) rather than choreography for monetary flows. This provides:

- **Strong ordering guarantees** - Steps execute sequentially in defined order
- **Centralized timeout/retry/compensation logic** - Single point of control
- **Auditability** - All state transitions are tracked in `saga_states` table

The `SagaCoordinator` (`services/transaction_service/app/events/saga_coordinator.py:222-333`) manages:
- Saga lifecycle (PENDING → IN_PROGRESS → COMPLETED/FAILED/COMPENSATED)
- Step execution with async/await patterns
- Automatic compensation on failure

### 1.3 Event Sourcing & CQRS

The platform follows **CQRS with Event Sourcing** (see `docs/architecture/cqrs_event_sourcing.md`):

- **Command Side**: Validates commands, applies domain logic, appends immutable events to PostgreSQL Event Store
- **Event Bus**: Kafka topic `crowdpay.events` distributes events via logical replication
- **Read Side**: Projectors consume events to update materialized views in ElasticSearch, Redis, ClickHouse

The `wallet_service/app/core/ledger.py` implements an **immutable, double-entry ledger** where:
- Every transfer creates two `LedgerEntry` records (DEBIT + CREDIT)
- Entries go through states: PENDING → COMMITTED → (optionally) REVERSED
- Changes are published as domain events (`ledger.entry.committed`)

### 1.4 Risk Assessment Architecture

The `risk_compliance_service` (`app/core/rule_engine.py` and `app/api/v1/assessment.py`) provides:

- **Rule Engine**: Evaluates configurable compliance rules (KYC status, amount thresholds, cross-border checks)
- **Decisions**: APPROVE, MANUAL_REVIEW, REJECT
- **Risk Levels**: LOW, MEDIUM, HIGH, CRITICAL
- **Async Assessment API**: Accepts requests, runs evaluation in background, publishes results via events

**Key Files Analyzed:**
- `docs/architecture/overview.md` - Macro architecture and service responsibilities
- `docs/architecture/saga_pattern.md` - Detailed Saga implementation with code examples
- `docs/architecture/cqrs_event_sourcing.md` - Event sourcing and CQRS patterns
- `services/transaction_service/app/sagas/payment_saga.py` - Payment Saga implementation
- `services/transaction_service/app/events/saga_coordinator.py` - Saga orchestration engine
- `services/transaction_service/app/models/saga_state.py` - Saga persistence model
- `services/transaction_service/app/repositories/saga_repository.py` - Saga database operations
- `services/wallet_service/app/core/ledger.py` - Ledger service and double-entry logic
- `services/wallet_service/app/events/consumer.py` - Event consumption patterns
- `services/risk_compliance_service/app/core/rule_engine.py` - Rule engine implementation
- `services/risk_compliance_service/app/api/v1/assessment.py` - Risk assessment API

---

## 2. Proposed Solution: Instant Settlement Architecture

### 2.1 Why Not Modify the Existing Saga?

**Critical Anti-Pattern to Avoid**: Injecting a synchronous, blocking API call to InstaPay directly inside the existing `PaymentSaga` would be an architectural anti-pattern because:

1. **Violates Saga Principles** - Sagas are designed for long-running, asynchronous distributed transactions with compensating actions. A blocking external call would compromise resilience.

2. **Timeout Complexity** - The existing Saga has a 5-minute TTL (`saga_coordinator.py:156`). A synchronous InstaPay call with its own timeout would create nested timeout management.

3. **Compensation Mismatch** - InstaPay's synchronous settlement cannot be cleanly compensated through the async compensation chain.

4. **Blocking Event Loop** - Synchronous calls would block the async event loop used by the Saga coordinator.

### 2.2 Proposed Pattern: Settlement Strategy

I propose implementing a **Strategy Pattern** to segregate the synchronous (instant) and asynchronous (standard) settlement flows at the point of transaction initiation.

```
                            ┌─────────────────────────┐
                            │  Transaction Request    │
                            │  (with settlement_type) │
                            └───────────┬─────────────┘
                                        │
                            ┌───────────▼─────────────┐
                            │  SettlementStrategy     │
                            │  Factory/Router         │
                            └───────────┬─────────────┘
                                        │
              ┌─────────────────────────┼─────────────────────────┐
              │                         │                         │
    ┌─────────▼─────────┐     ┌────────▼────────┐     ┌──────────▼────────┐
    │  StandardSettlement│     │  InstantSettlement│     │  Future Strategies│
    │  Strategy          │     │  Strategy          │     │  (e.g., Scheduled) │
    │  (Existing Saga)   │     │  (New Sync Flow)   │     │                    │
    └────────────────────┘     └───────────────────┘     └────────────────────┘
```

### 2.3 InstantSettlementStrategy Design

The `InstantSettlementStrategy` will orchestrate a **synchronous, blocking sequence** of calls that is self-contained and separate from the async Saga:

```python
# services/transaction_service/app/strategies/instant_settlement.py

class InstantSettlementStrategy:
    """
    Synchronous settlement strategy for premium instant settlements.
    Executes a deterministic sequence with compensating transactions on failure.
    """

    def __init__(
        self,
        risk_service: RiskService,
        wallet_service: WalletService,
        instapay_client: InstaPayClient,
        event_publisher: EventPublisher,
    ):
        self.risk = risk_service
        self.wallet = wallet_service
        self.instapay = instapay_client
        self.publisher = event_publisher

    async def execute(self, command: InstantPaymentCommand) -> SettlementResult:
        """
        Execute synchronous instant settlement flow.

        Flow:
        1. Pre-authorization risk check (synchronous)
        2. Reserve funds in payer's wallet
        3. Call InstaPay API (synchronous external call)
        4. On success: Publish InstantSettlementSucceeded event
        5. On failure: Execute compensation (release reserved funds)
        """
        reservation_id = None

        try:
            # Step 1: Synchronous pre-authorization risk check
            risk_result = await self._pre_auth_risk_check(command)
            if risk_result.decision == Decision.REJECT:
                raise InstantSettlementRejected("Risk check failed")

            # Step 2: Reserve funds (with PENDING status)
            reservation_id = await self._reserve_funds(command)

            # Step 3: Synchronous InstaPay API call
            instapay_response = await self._call_instapay(command)

            # Step 4: Publish success event (triggers wallet ledger update)
            await self._publish_success_event(command, instapay_response, reservation_id)

            return SettlementResult(
                status=SettlementStatus.COMPLETED,
                transaction_id=command.transaction_id,
                instapay_reference=instapay_response.reference,
            )

        except InstaPayError as e:
            # Compensation: Release reserved funds
            if reservation_id:
                await self._release_reserved_funds(reservation_id, command)
            raise InstantSettlementFailed(f"InstaPay call failed: {e}")
```

### 2.4 Transaction Service Modifications

The `transaction_service` needs a router/factory to determine which strategy to use:

```python
# services/transaction_service/app/services/settlement_router.py

class SettlementRouter:
    """
    Routes payment requests to appropriate settlement strategy.
    """

    def __init__(
        self,
        standard_strategy: StandardSettlementStrategy,  # Wraps existing PaymentSaga
        instant_strategy: InstantSettlementStrategy,
    ):
        self.strategies = {
            SettlementType.STANDARD: standard_strategy,
            SettlementType.INSTANT: instant_strategy,
        }

    async def route(self, command: PaymentCommand) -> SettlementResult:
        """
        Determine and execute appropriate settlement strategy.
        """
        settlement_type = self._determine_settlement_type(command)
        strategy = self.strategies[settlement_type]
        return await strategy.execute(command)

    def _determine_settlement_type(self, command: PaymentCommand) -> SettlementType:
        """
        Business rules for settlement type selection:
        - User must be premium tier
        - Transaction must be eligible (amount limits, currency support)
        - Feature flag enabled
        """
        if (
            command.user_tier == UserTier.PREMIUM and
            command.request_instant_settlement and
            self._is_instant_eligible(command)
        ):
            return SettlementType.INSTANT
        return SettlementType.STANDARD
```

### 2.5 InstaPay API Integration

The InstaPay integration should be wrapped in a dedicated client with:

```python
# services/transaction_service/app/integrations/instapay_client.py

class InstaPayClient:
    """
    HTTP client for InstaPay external API.
    Implements circuit breaker, retries, and timeout handling.
    """

    def __init__(
        self,
        base_url: str,
        api_key: str,
        timeout: float = 10.0,  # InstaPay SLA
    ):
        self.base_url = base_url
        self.timeout = timeout
        self._circuit_breaker = CircuitBreaker(
            failure_threshold=5,
            recovery_timeout=60,
        )

    @circuit_breaker_decorator
    async def settle_instantly(
        self,
        transaction_id: str,
        amount: Money,
        payer_account: str,
        payee_account: str,
    ) -> InstaPayResponse:
        """
        Synchronous call to InstaPay settlement API.
        """
        async with httpx.AsyncClient(timeout=self.timeout) as client:
            response = await client.post(
                f"{self.base_url}/v1/instant-settle",
                json={
                    "transaction_id": transaction_id,
                    "amount": str(amount.value),
                    "currency": amount.currency,
                    "payer_account": payer_account,
                    "payee_account": payee_account,
                },
                headers={"Authorization": f"Bearer {self.api_key}"},
            )
            response.raise_for_status()
            return InstaPayResponse.parse_obj(response.json())
```

### 2.6 Wallet Service Ledger Updates

**Critical Design Decision**: To preserve the event-sourced nature of the wallet ledger, the `InstantSettlementStrategy` should **NOT** directly call wallet APIs to finalize ledger entries. Instead:

1. The `InstantSettlementStrategy` publishes an **`InstantSettlementSucceeded`** event
2. The `wallet_service` has a new event consumer that handles this trusted event
3. The consumer finalizes the ledger entries (PENDING → COMMITTED)

```python
# services/wallet_service/app/events/instant_settlement_consumer.py

class InstantSettlementEventHandler:
    """
    Handles InstantSettlementSucceeded events to finalize ledger entries.
    """

    async def handle_instant_settlement_succeeded(
        self,
        event: InstantSettlementSucceeded,
    ) -> None:
        """
        Finalize ledger entries after successful instant settlement.

        This event is highly trusted (comes from authenticated internal service)
        and triggers the final state transition.
        """
        # Mark reserved funds as committed
        await self.ledger_repo.mark_committed(
            entry_ids=[event.data["debit_entry_id"]],
        )

        # Credit the payee
        credit_entry = LedgerEntry(
            id=uuid.uuid4(),
            wallet_id=event.data["payee_wallet_id"],
            entry_type=EntryType.CREDIT,
            money=Money(
                amount=Decimal(event.data["amount"]),
                currency=Currency(event.data["currency"]),
            ),
            external_txn_id=event.data["transaction_id"],
            status=EntryStatus.COMMITTED,  # Directly committed
        )
        await self.ledger_repo.save_entries([credit_entry])

        # Publish ledger event for downstream projections
        await self.publisher.publish(
            "ledger.instant.settled",
            {
                "transaction_id": event.data["transaction_id"],
                "instapay_reference": event.data["instapay_reference"],
                "settled_at": datetime.utcnow().isoformat(),
            },
        )
```

**New Event Types Required:**
```python
# Add to wallet_service/app/events/consumer.py _EVENT_REGISTRY

class InstantSettlementSucceeded(Event):
    type: str = "instant.settlement.succeeded"
    data: Dict[str, Any]  # { transaction_id, debit_entry_id, payee_wallet_id, amount, currency, instapay_reference }

class InstantSettlementFailed(Event):
    type: str = "instant.settlement.failed"
    data: Dict[str, Any]  # { transaction_id, reservation_id, reason }
```

### 2.7 Risk & Compliance Service Modifications

The `risk_compliance_service` needs enhancements for instant settlement scenarios:

**2.7.1 New Rule Set for Instant Settlements**

Instant settlements carry higher risk due to their irrevocable nature. New rules should be added:

```yaml
# config/rules/instant_settlement_rules.yaml
- name: "InstantSettlement_HighAmount"
  description: "Flag instant settlements over $5k for manual review"
  priority: 100
  conditions:
    - "transaction.settlement_type == 'INSTANT'"
    - "transaction.amount > 5000"
  actions:
    risk_level: "HIGH"
    decision: "MANUAL_REVIEW"
    alert_code: "INST-001"

- name: "InstantSettlement_NewUser"
  description: "Reject instant settlements for users < 30 days old"
  priority: 110
  conditions:
    - "transaction.settlement_type == 'INSTANT'"
    - "user.account_age_days < 30"
  actions:
    risk_level: "CRITICAL"
    decision: "REJECT"
    alert_code: "INST-002"

- name: "InstantSettlement_VelocityCheck"
  description: "Flag rapid successive instant settlements"
  priority: 95
  conditions:
    - "transaction.settlement_type == 'INSTANT'"
    - "user.instant_settlements_24h > 3"
  actions:
    risk_level: "HIGH"
    decision: "MANUAL_REVIEW"
    alert_code: "INST-003"
```

**2.7.2 Synchronous Risk Assessment Endpoint**

For instant settlements, we need a **synchronous** risk check (not the async background job pattern in current `assessment.py`):

```python
# services/risk_compliance_service/app/api/v1/instant_assessment.py

@router.post(
    "/instant",
    response_model=InstantAssessmentOut,
    status_code=status.HTTP_200_OK,  # Synchronous response
    summary="Synchronous risk assessment for instant settlements",
)
async def instant_assessment(
    payload: InstantAssessmentIn,
    engine: _RiskEngine = Depends(get_risk_engine),
) -> InstantAssessmentOut:
    """
    Synchronous risk assessment designed for instant settlement flows.
    Must respond within 500ms SLA.
    """
    # Apply instant-settlement-specific rules
    score = engine.score_instant(
        amount=payload.amount,
        currency_code=payload.currency_code,
        user_reputation=_fetch_user_reputation(payload.user_id),
        settlement_type="INSTANT",
    )

    decision = engine.decide_instant(score)

    return InstantAssessmentOut(
        decision=decision,
        score=score,
        reference_id=payload.reference_id,
    )
```

---

## 3. Failure Handling & Compensation Strategy

### 3.1 Failure Scenarios

The InstantSettlementStrategy must handle failures at each stage:

| Failure Point | State | Compensation Action |
|---------------|-------|---------------------|
| Pre-auth risk check fails | No funds reserved | None - reject transaction |
| Funds reservation fails | No funds reserved | None - insufficient funds error |
| InstaPay API fails | Funds reserved (PENDING) | Release reserved funds |
| InstaPay timeout | Funds reserved, unknown external state | Manual reconciliation queue |
| Event publish fails | Settlement complete externally | Retry event publish with idempotency |

### 3.2 Compensation Implementation

```python
# services/transaction_service/app/strategies/instant_settlement.py

class InstantSettlementStrategy:

    async def _release_reserved_funds(
        self,
        reservation_id: str,
        command: InstantPaymentCommand,
    ) -> None:
        """
        Compensation action: Release reserved funds back to payer.
        Published as event for eventual consistency.
        """
        await self.publisher.publish(
            event_name="instant.settlement.compensation.release_funds",
            payload={
                "reservation_id": reservation_id,
                "transaction_id": command.transaction_id,
                "payer_wallet_id": command.payer_wallet_id,
                "amount": str(command.amount.value),
                "currency": command.amount.currency,
                "reason": "InstaPay settlement failed",
            },
        )

        # For critical path, also make synchronous call to ensure immediate release
        await self.wallet.release_reservation(reservation_id)

    async def _handle_instapay_timeout(
        self,
        command: InstantPaymentCommand,
        reservation_id: str,
    ) -> None:
        """
        Handle InstaPay timeout - uncertain external state.
        Queue for manual reconciliation.
        """
        await self.publisher.publish(
            event_name="instant.settlement.reconciliation.required",
            payload={
                "transaction_id": command.transaction_id,
                "reservation_id": reservation_id,
                "status": "TIMEOUT",
                "requires_manual_check": True,
            },
        )

        # Mark transaction as PENDING_RECONCILIATION
        raise InstantSettlementTimeout(
            "InstaPay timeout - queued for reconciliation"
        )
```

### 3.3 Wallet Service Compensation Handler

```python
# services/wallet_service/app/events/instant_settlement_consumer.py

async def handle_instant_settlement_compensation(
    self,
    event: InstantSettlementCompensation,
) -> None:
    """
    Handle compensation event - release reserved funds.
    """
    reservation_id = event.data["reservation_id"]

    # Mark the PENDING ledger entry as REVERSED
    await self.ledger_repo.mark_reversed(
        entry_ids=[reservation_id],
        reason=event.data["reason"],
        failed=True,
    )

    # Publish compensation complete event
    await self.publisher.publish(
        "ledger.reservation.released",
        {
            "reservation_id": reservation_id,
            "transaction_id": event.data["transaction_id"],
            "released_at": datetime.utcnow().isoformat(),
        },
    )
```

---

## 4. Sequence Diagram: Successful Instant Settlement

```mermaid
sequenceDiagram
    autonumber
    actor User
    participant API as API Gateway
    participant TXN as Transaction Service<br/>(SettlementRouter)
    participant INST as InstantSettlement<br/>Strategy
    participant RISK as Risk & Compliance<br/>Service
    participant WALLET as Wallet Service
    participant INSTAPAY as InstaPay<br/>(External)
    participant KAFKA as Event Bus<br/>(Kafka)

    User->>API: POST /transactions<br/>{settlement_type: "INSTANT"}
    API->>TXN: route(PaymentCommand)

    Note over TXN: Determine strategy based on<br/>user tier & eligibility

    TXN->>INST: execute(InstantPaymentCommand)

    rect rgb(240, 248, 255)
        Note over INST,RISK: Step 1: Synchronous Risk Check
        INST->>RISK: POST /api/v1/assessment/instant
        RISK-->>INST: {decision: APPROVED, score: 0.2}
    end

    rect rgb(240, 255, 240)
        Note over INST,WALLET: Step 2: Reserve Funds
        INST->>WALLET: POST /wallet/v1/reserve
        WALLET-->>INST: {reservation_id: "res_123",<br/>status: PENDING}
    end

    rect rgb(255, 248, 240)
        Note over INST,INSTAPAY: Step 3: Synchronous External Settlement
        INST->>INSTAPAY: POST /v1/instant-settle
        Note over INSTAPAY: Process settlement<br/>in real-time
        INSTAPAY-->>INST: {reference: "IP_456",<br/>status: SETTLED}
    end

    rect rgb(248, 240, 255)
        Note over INST,KAFKA: Step 4: Publish Success Event
        INST->>KAFKA: publish(InstantSettlementSucceeded)
        KAFKA-->>INST: ack
    end

    INST-->>TXN: SettlementResult{COMPLETED}
    TXN-->>API: 200 OK
    API-->>User: Transaction Settled<br/>{instapay_ref: "IP_456"}

    Note over KAFKA,WALLET: Async: Event-Driven Ledger Update

    KAFKA->>WALLET: consume(InstantSettlementSucceeded)

    rect rgb(240, 255, 240)
        Note over WALLET: Finalize Ledger Entries
        WALLET->>WALLET: mark_committed(debit_entry)
        WALLET->>WALLET: create_credit_entry(COMMITTED)
    end

    WALLET->>KAFKA: publish(ledger.instant.settled)
```

---

## 5. Implementation Roadmap

### Phase 1: Foundation (Sprint 1-2)
- [ ] Add `settlement_type` field to `PaymentCommand` model
- [ ] Implement `SettlementRouter` with strategy factory
- [ ] Create `StandardSettlementStrategy` wrapper around existing `PaymentSaga`
- [ ] Add database migrations for instant settlement metadata

### Phase 2: InstaPay Integration (Sprint 3-4)
- [ ] Implement `InstaPayClient` with circuit breaker
- [ ] Create `InstantSettlementStrategy` class
- [ ] Add InstaPay configuration and credentials management
- [ ] Implement synchronous risk assessment endpoint

### Phase 3: Wallet Service Updates (Sprint 4-5)
- [ ] Add `InstantSettlementSucceeded` event handler
- [ ] Implement compensation handlers for fund release
- [ ] Add new event types to consumer registry
- [ ] Update ledger service for instant settlement entries

### Phase 4: Risk & Compliance (Sprint 5-6)
- [ ] Create instant settlement rule set
- [ ] Implement velocity checking for instant settlements
- [ ] Add synchronous assessment endpoint
- [ ] Configure stricter thresholds for instant payments

### Phase 5: Testing & Rollout (Sprint 6-7)
- [ ] Integration tests with InstaPay sandbox
- [ ] Chaos testing for failure scenarios
- [ ] Performance testing for latency SLAs
- [ ] Gradual rollout with feature flags

---

## 6. Summary of Key Architectural Decisions

| Decision | Rationale |
|----------|-----------|
| **Separate Strategy** instead of modifying Saga | Preserves async resilience of existing flow; avoids anti-pattern of blocking calls in Saga |
| **Synchronous Risk Check** for instant path | Instant settlements require immediate decision; cannot wait for async background processing |
| **Event-driven Ledger Update** | Preserves event-sourcing integrity; wallet service remains single source of truth for ledger |
| **Compensation via Events** | Ensures auditability; enables retry and idempotency; aligns with existing patterns |
| **Circuit Breaker** for InstaPay | Protects system from external service degradation; enables graceful fallback |
| **Stricter Risk Rules** for instant | Irrevocable nature of instant settlements requires higher scrutiny |

---

| `services/risk_compliance_service/app/api/v1/assessment.py` | Risk assessment API |

# INSTANT_SETTLEMENT_PROPOSAL.md

## Architectural Proposal: "Instant Settlements" Feature for CrowdPay Connect

**Author:** Lead Architect
**Version:** 1.0
**Date:** 2026-02-02
**Status:** Draft - For Review

---

## Executive Summary

This document proposes an architectural approach to integrate the "Instant Settlement" premium feature into CrowdPay Connect, enabling select users to have their transactions settled in near real-time through integration with a new third-party provider, **InstaPay**. The proposal addresses how to incorporate this synchronous settlement path while preserving the platform's existing architectural principles: event-driven design, Saga-based transaction orchestration, and event-sourced ledger integrity.

---

## 1. Analysis of the Current Architecture

### 1.1 Standard Payment Settlement Flow

The current CrowdPay Connect platform processes payments through a **distributed Saga pattern** with multiple asynchronous steps designed for resilience and standard settlement timelines (T+2 days). The flow is orchestrated by the `transaction_service` and involves several bounded contexts:

**Key Services Involved:**
1. **Transaction Service** - Orchestrates the payment Saga, manages saga state, publishes domain events
2. **Wallet Service** - Manages wallet balances via an immutable, append-only double-entry ledger
3. **Risk & Compliance Service** - Performs real-time KYC, AML, and fraud checks
4. **KYC Service** - Verifies customer identity
5. **FX Service** - Handles currency conversion for multi-currency payments

**Current Payment Saga Steps** (as defined in `services/transaction_service/app/sagas/payment_saga.py:238-273`):
```
1. KYC Verification      → Verify payer has valid KYC profile
2. Risk Assessment       → Real-time transaction risk evaluation
3. Debit Payer          → Reserve/debit funds from payer's wallet
4. Currency Conversion   → Convert if source/target currencies differ
5. Credit CrowdPod      → Credit funds to destination wallet
6. Settlement           → Mark transaction as settled (T+2 timeline)
```

Each step has an associated **compensation action** that executes in reverse order if any step fails, ensuring eventual consistency (see `payment_saga.py:275-306`).

### 1.2 Saga Pattern Implementation

The platform uses an **orchestration-based Saga pattern** (as documented in `docs/architecture/saga_pattern.md`) rather than choreography for monetary flows. This provides:

- **Strong ordering guarantees** - Steps execute sequentially in defined order
- **Centralized timeout/retry/compensation logic** - Single point of control
- **Auditability** - All state transitions are tracked in `saga_states` table

The `SagaCoordinator` (`services/transaction_service/app/events/saga_coordinator.py:222-333`) manages:
- Saga lifecycle (PENDING → IN_PROGRESS → COMPLETED/FAILED/COMPENSATED)
- Step execution with async/await patterns
- Automatic compensation on failure

### 1.3 Event Sourcing & CQRS

The platform follows **CQRS with Event Sourcing** (see `docs/architecture/cqrs_event_sourcing.md`):

- **Command Side**: Validates commands, applies domain logic, appends immutable events to PostgreSQL Event Store
- **Event Bus**: Kafka topic `crowdpay.events` distributes events via logical replication
- **Read Side**: Projectors consume events to update materialized views in ElasticSearch, Redis, ClickHouse

The `wallet_service/app/core/ledger.py` implements an **immutable, double-entry ledger** where:
- Every transfer creates two `LedgerEntry` records (DEBIT + CREDIT)
- Entries go through states: PENDING → COMMITTED → (optionally) REVERSED
- Changes are published as domain events (`ledger.entry.committed`)

### 1.4 Risk Assessment Architecture

The `risk_compliance_service` (`app/core/rule_engine.py` and `app/api/v1/assessment.py`) provides:

- **Rule Engine**: Evaluates configurable compliance rules (KYC status, amount thresholds, cross-border checks)
- **Decisions**: APPROVE, MANUAL_REVIEW, REJECT
- **Risk Levels**: LOW, MEDIUM, HIGH, CRITICAL
- **Async Assessment API**: Accepts requests, runs evaluation in background, publishes results via events

**Key Files Analyzed:**
- `docs/architecture/overview.md` - Macro architecture and service responsibilities
- `docs/architecture/saga_pattern.md` - Detailed Saga implementation with code examples
- `docs/architecture/cqrs_event_sourcing.md` - Event sourcing and CQRS patterns
- `services/transaction_service/app/sagas/payment_saga.py` - Payment Saga implementation
- `services/transaction_service/app/events/saga_coordinator.py` - Saga orchestration engine
- `services/transaction_service/app/models/saga_state.py` - Saga persistence model
- `services/transaction_service/app/repositories/saga_repository.py` - Saga database operations
- `services/wallet_service/app/core/ledger.py` - Ledger service and double-entry logic
- `services/wallet_service/app/events/consumer.py` - Event consumption patterns
- `services/risk_compliance_service/app/core/rule_engine.py` - Rule engine implementation
- `services/risk_compliance_service/app/api/v1/assessment.py` - Risk assessment API

---

## 2. Proposed Solution: Instant Settlement Architecture

### 2.1 Why Not Modify the Existing Saga?

**Critical Anti-Pattern to Avoid**: Injecting a synchronous, blocking API call to InstaPay directly inside the existing `PaymentSaga` would be an architectural anti-pattern because:

1. **Violates Saga Principles** - Sagas are designed for long-running, asynchronous distributed transactions with compensating actions. A blocking external call would compromise resilience.

2. **Timeout Complexity** - The existing Saga has a 5-minute TTL (`saga_coordinator.py:156`). A synchronous InstaPay call with its own timeout would create nested timeout management.

3. **Compensation Mismatch** - InstaPay's synchronous settlement cannot be cleanly compensated through the async compensation chain.

4. **Blocking Event Loop** - Synchronous calls would block the async event loop used by the Saga coordinator.

### 2.2 Proposed Pattern: Settlement Strategy

I propose implementing a **Strategy Pattern** to segregate the synchronous (instant) and asynchronous (standard) settlement flows at the point of transaction initiation.

```
                            ┌─────────────────────────┐
                            │  Transaction Request    │
                            │  (with settlement_type) │
                            └───────────┬─────────────┘
                                        │
                            ┌───────────▼─────────────┐
                            │  SettlementStrategy     │
                            │  Factory/Router         │
                            └───────────┬─────────────┘
                                        │
              ┌─────────────────────────┼─────────────────────────┐
              │                         │                         │
    ┌─────────▼─────────┐     ┌────────▼────────┐     ┌──────────▼────────┐
    │  StandardSettlement│     │  InstantSettlement│     │  Future Strategies│
    │  Strategy          │     │  Strategy          │     │  (e.g., Scheduled) │
    │  (Existing Saga)   │     │  (New Sync Flow)   │     │                    │
    └────────────────────┘     └───────────────────┘     └────────────────────┘
```

### 2.3 InstantSettlementStrategy Design

The `InstantSettlementStrategy` will orchestrate a **synchronous, blocking sequence** of calls that is self-contained and separate from the async Saga:

```python
# services/transaction_service/app/strategies/instant_settlement.py

class InstantSettlementStrategy:
    """
    Synchronous settlement strategy for premium instant settlements.
    Executes a deterministic sequence with compensating transactions on failure.
    """

    def __init__(
        self,
        risk_service: RiskService,
        wallet_service: WalletService,
        instapay_client: InstaPayClient,
        event_publisher: EventPublisher,
    ):
        self.risk = risk_service
        self.wallet = wallet_service
        self.instapay = instapay_client
        self.publisher = event_publisher

    async def execute(self, command: InstantPaymentCommand) -> SettlementResult:
        """
        Execute synchronous instant settlement flow.

        Flow:
        1. Pre-authorization risk check (synchronous)
        2. Reserve funds in payer's wallet
        3. Call InstaPay API (synchronous external call)
        4. On success: Publish InstantSettlementSucceeded event
        5. On failure: Execute compensation (release reserved funds)
        """
        reservation_id = None

        try:
            # Step 1: Synchronous pre-authorization risk check
            risk_result = await self._pre_auth_risk_check(command)
            if risk_result.decision == Decision.REJECT:
                raise InstantSettlementRejected("Risk check failed")

            # Step 2: Reserve funds (with PENDING status)
            reservation_id = await self._reserve_funds(command)

            # Step 3: Synchronous InstaPay API call
            instapay_response = await self._call_instapay(command)

            # Step 4: Publish success event (triggers wallet ledger update)
            await self._publish_success_event(command, instapay_response, reservation_id)

            return SettlementResult(
                status=SettlementStatus.COMPLETED,
                transaction_id=command.transaction_id,
                instapay_reference=instapay_response.reference,
            )

        except InstaPayError as e:
            # Compensation: Release reserved funds
            if reservation_id:
                await self._release_reserved_funds(reservation_id, command)
            raise InstantSettlementFailed(f"InstaPay call failed: {e}")
```

### 2.4 Transaction Service Modifications

The `transaction_service` needs a router/factory to determine which strategy to use:

```python
# services/transaction_service/app/services/settlement_router.py

class SettlementRouter:
    """
    Routes payment requests to appropriate settlement strategy.
    """

    def __init__(
        self,
        standard_strategy: StandardSettlementStrategy,  # Wraps existing PaymentSaga
        instant_strategy: InstantSettlementStrategy,
    ):
        self.strategies = {
            SettlementType.STANDARD: standard_strategy,
            SettlementType.INSTANT: instant_strategy,
        }

    async def route(self, command: PaymentCommand) -> SettlementResult:
        """
        Determine and execute appropriate settlement strategy.
        """
        settlement_type = self._determine_settlement_type(command)
        strategy = self.strategies[settlement_type]
        return await strategy.execute(command)

    def _determine_settlement_type(self, command: PaymentCommand) -> SettlementType:
        """
        Business rules for settlement type selection:
        - User must be premium tier
        - Transaction must be eligible (amount limits, currency support)
        - Feature flag enabled
        """
        if (
            command.user_tier == UserTier.PREMIUM and
            command.request_instant_settlement and
            self._is_instant_eligible(command)
        ):
            return SettlementType.INSTANT
        return SettlementType.STANDARD
```

### 2.5 InstaPay API Integration

The InstaPay integration should be wrapped in a dedicated client with:

```python
# services/transaction_service/app/integrations/instapay_client.py

class InstaPayClient:
    """
    HTTP client for InstaPay external API.
    Implements circuit breaker, retries, and timeout handling.
    """

    def __init__(
        self,
        base_url: str,
        api_key: str,
        timeout: float = 10.0,  # InstaPay SLA
    ):
        self.base_url = base_url
        self.timeout = timeout
        self._circuit_breaker = CircuitBreaker(
            failure_threshold=5,
            recovery_timeout=60,
        )

    @circuit_breaker_decorator
    async def settle_instantly(
        self,
        transaction_id: str,
        amount: Money,
        payer_account: str,
        payee_account: str,
    ) -> InstaPayResponse:
        """
        Synchronous call to InstaPay settlement API.
        """
        async with httpx.AsyncClient(timeout=self.timeout) as client:
            response = await client.post(
                f"{self.base_url}/v1/instant-settle",
                json={
                    "transaction_id": transaction_id,
                    "amount": str(amount.value),
                    "currency": amount.currency,
                    "payer_account": payer_account,
                    "payee_account": payee_account,
                },
                headers={"Authorization": f"Bearer {self.api_key}"},
            )
            response.raise_for_status()
            return InstaPayResponse.parse_obj(response.json())
```

### 2.6 Wallet Service Ledger Updates

**Critical Design Decision**: To preserve the event-sourced nature of the wallet ledger, the `InstantSettlementStrategy` should **NOT** directly call wallet APIs to finalize ledger entries. Instead:

1. The `InstantSettlementStrategy` publishes an **`InstantSettlementSucceeded`** event
2. The `wallet_service` has a new event consumer that handles this trusted event
3. The consumer finalizes the ledger entries (PENDING → COMMITTED)

```python
# services/wallet_service/app/events/instant_settlement_consumer.py

class InstantSettlementEventHandler:
    """
    Handles InstantSettlementSucceeded events to finalize ledger entries.
    """

    async def handle_instant_settlement_succeeded(
        self,
        event: InstantSettlementSucceeded,
    ) -> None:
        """
        Finalize ledger entries after successful instant settlement.

        This event is highly trusted (comes from authenticated internal service)
        and triggers the final state transition.
        """
        # Mark reserved funds as committed
        await self.ledger_repo.mark_committed(
            entry_ids=[event.data["debit_entry_id"]],
        )

        # Credit the payee
        credit_entry = LedgerEntry(
            id=uuid.uuid4(),
            wallet_id=event.data["payee_wallet_id"],
            entry_type=EntryType.CREDIT,
            money=Money(
                amount=Decimal(event.data["amount"]),
                currency=Currency(event.data["currency"]),
            ),
            external_txn_id=event.data["transaction_id"],
            status=EntryStatus.COMMITTED,  # Directly committed
        )
        await self.ledger_repo.save_entries([credit_entry])

        # Publish ledger event for downstream projections
        await self.publisher.publish(
            "ledger.instant.settled",
            {
                "transaction_id": event.data["transaction_id"],
                "instapay_reference": event.data["instapay_reference"],
                "settled_at": datetime.utcnow().isoformat(),
            },
        )
```

**New Event Types Required:**
```python
# Add to wallet_service/app/events/consumer.py _EVENT_REGISTRY

class InstantSettlementSucceeded(Event):
    type: str = "instant.settlement.succeeded"
    data: Dict[str, Any]  # { transaction_id, debit_entry_id, payee_wallet_id, amount, currency, instapay_reference }

class InstantSettlementFailed(Event):
    type: str = "instant.settlement.failed"
    data: Dict[str, Any]  # { transaction_id, reservation_id, reason }
```

### 2.7 Risk & Compliance Service Modifications

The `risk_compliance_service` needs enhancements for instant settlement scenarios:

**2.7.1 New Rule Set for Instant Settlements**

Instant settlements carry higher risk due to their irrevocable nature. New rules should be added:

```yaml
# config/rules/instant_settlement_rules.yaml
- name: "InstantSettlement_HighAmount"
  description: "Flag instant settlements over $5k for manual review"
  priority: 100
  conditions:
    - "transaction.settlement_type == 'INSTANT'"
    - "transaction.amount > 5000"
  actions:
    risk_level: "HIGH"
    decision: "MANUAL_REVIEW"
    alert_code: "INST-001"

- name: "InstantSettlement_NewUser"
  description: "Reject instant settlements for users < 30 days old"
  priority: 110
  conditions:
    - "transaction.settlement_type == 'INSTANT'"
    - "user.account_age_days < 30"
  actions:
    risk_level: "CRITICAL"
    decision: "REJECT"
    alert_code: "INST-002"

- name: "InstantSettlement_VelocityCheck"
  description: "Flag rapid successive instant settlements"
  priority: 95
  conditions:
    - "transaction.settlement_type == 'INSTANT'"
    - "user.instant_settlements_24h > 3"
  actions:
    risk_level: "HIGH"
    decision: "MANUAL_REVIEW"
    alert_code: "INST-003"
```

**2.7.2 Synchronous Risk Assessment Endpoint**

For instant settlements, we need a **synchronous** risk check (not the async background job pattern in current `assessment.py`):

```python
# services/risk_compliance_service/app/api/v1/instant_assessment.py

@router.post(
    "/instant",
    response_model=InstantAssessmentOut,
    status_code=status.HTTP_200_OK,  # Synchronous response
    summary="Synchronous risk assessment for instant settlements",
)
async def instant_assessment(
    payload: InstantAssessmentIn,
    engine: _RiskEngine = Depends(get_risk_engine),
) -> InstantAssessmentOut:
    """
    Synchronous risk assessment designed for instant settlement flows.
    Must respond within 500ms SLA.
    """
    # Apply instant-settlement-specific rules
    score = engine.score_instant(
        amount=payload.amount,
        currency_code=payload.currency_code,
        user_reputation=_fetch_user_reputation(payload.user_id),
        settlement_type="INSTANT",
    )

    decision = engine.decide_instant(score)

    return InstantAssessmentOut(
        decision=decision,
        score=score,
        reference_id=payload.reference_id,
    )
```

---

## 3. Failure Handling & Compensation Strategy

### 3.1 Failure Scenarios

The InstantSettlementStrategy must handle failures at each stage:

| Failure Point | State | Compensation Action |
|---------------|-------|---------------------|
| Pre-auth risk check fails | No funds reserved | None - reject transaction |
| Funds reservation fails | No funds reserved | None - insufficient funds error |
| InstaPay API fails | Funds reserved (PENDING) | Release reserved funds |
| InstaPay timeout | Funds reserved, unknown external state | Manual reconciliation queue |
| Event publish fails | Settlement complete externally | Retry event publish with idempotency |

### 3.2 Compensation Implementation

```python
# services/transaction_service/app/strategies/instant_settlement.py

class InstantSettlementStrategy:

    async def _release_reserved_funds(
        self,
        reservation_id: str,
        command: InstantPaymentCommand,
    ) -> None:
        """
        Compensation action: Release reserved funds back to payer.
        Published as event for eventual consistency.
        """
        await self.publisher.publish(
            event_name="instant.settlement.compensation.release_funds",
            payload={
                "reservation_id": reservation_id,
                "transaction_id": command.transaction_id,
                "payer_wallet_id": command.payer_wallet_id,
                "amount": str(command.amount.value),
                "currency": command.amount.currency,
                "reason": "InstaPay settlement failed",
            },
        )

        # For critical path, also make synchronous call to ensure immediate release
        await self.wallet.release_reservation(reservation_id)

    async def _handle_instapay_timeout(
        self,
        command: InstantPaymentCommand,
        reservation_id: str,
    ) -> None:
        """
        Handle InstaPay timeout - uncertain external state.
        Queue for manual reconciliation.
        """
        await self.publisher.publish(
            event_name="instant.settlement.reconciliation.required",
            payload={
                "transaction_id": command.transaction_id,
                "reservation_id": reservation_id,
                "status": "TIMEOUT",
                "requires_manual_check": True,
            },
        )

        # Mark transaction as PENDING_RECONCILIATION
        raise InstantSettlementTimeout(
            "InstaPay timeout - queued for reconciliation"
        )
```

### 3.3 Wallet Service Compensation Handler

```python
# services/wallet_service/app/events/instant_settlement_consumer.py

async def handle_instant_settlement_compensation(
    self,
    event: InstantSettlementCompensation,
) -> None:
    """
    Handle compensation event - release reserved funds.
    """
    reservation_id = event.data["reservation_id"]

    # Mark the PENDING ledger entry as REVERSED
    await self.ledger_repo.mark_reversed(
        entry_ids=[reservation_id],
        reason=event.data["reason"],
        failed=True,
    )

    # Publish compensation complete event
    await self.publisher.publish(
        "ledger.reservation.released",
        {
            "reservation_id": reservation_id,
            "transaction_id": event.data["transaction_id"],
            "released_at": datetime.utcnow().isoformat(),
        },
    )
```

---

## 4. Sequence Diagram: Successful Instant Settlement

```mermaid
sequenceDiagram
    autonumber
    actor User
    participant API as API Gateway
    participant TXN as Transaction Service<br/>(SettlementRouter)
    participant INST as InstantSettlement<br/>Strategy
    participant RISK as Risk & Compliance<br/>Service
    participant WALLET as Wallet Service
    participant INSTAPAY as InstaPay<br/>(External)
    participant KAFKA as Event Bus<br/>(Kafka)

    User->>API: POST /transactions<br/>{settlement_type: "INSTANT"}
    API->>TXN: route(PaymentCommand)

    Note over TXN: Determine strategy based on<br/>user tier & eligibility

    TXN->>INST: execute(InstantPaymentCommand)

    rect rgb(240, 248, 255)
        Note over INST,RISK: Step 1: Synchronous Risk Check
        INST->>RISK: POST /api/v1/assessment/instant
        RISK-->>INST: {decision: APPROVED, score: 0.2}
    end

    rect rgb(240, 255, 240)
        Note over INST,WALLET: Step 2: Reserve Funds
        INST->>WALLET: POST /wallet/v1/reserve
        WALLET-->>INST: {reservation_id: "res_123",<br/>status: PENDING}
    end

    rect rgb(255, 248, 240)
        Note over INST,INSTAPAY: Step 3: Synchronous External Settlement
        INST->>INSTAPAY: POST /v1/instant-settle
        Note over INSTAPAY: Process settlement<br/>in real-time
        INSTAPAY-->>INST: {reference: "IP_456",<br/>status: SETTLED}
    end

    rect rgb(248, 240, 255)
        Note over INST,KAFKA: Step 4: Publish Success Event
        INST->>KAFKA: publish(InstantSettlementSucceeded)
        KAFKA-->>INST: ack
    end

    INST-->>TXN: SettlementResult{COMPLETED}
    TXN-->>API: 200 OK
    API-->>User: Transaction Settled<br/>{instapay_ref: "IP_456"}

    Note over KAFKA,WALLET: Async: Event-Driven Ledger Update

    KAFKA->>WALLET: consume(InstantSettlementSucceeded)

    rect rgb(240, 255, 240)
        Note over WALLET: Finalize Ledger Entries
        WALLET->>WALLET: mark_committed(debit_entry)
        WALLET->>WALLET: create_credit_entry(COMMITTED)
    end

    WALLET->>KAFKA: publish(ledger.instant.settled)
```

---

## 5. Implementation Roadmap

### Phase 1: Foundation (Sprint 1-2)
- [ ] Add `settlement_type` field to `PaymentCommand` model
- [ ] Implement `SettlementRouter` with strategy factory
- [ ] Create `StandardSettlementStrategy` wrapper around existing `PaymentSaga`
- [ ] Add database migrations for instant settlement metadata

### Phase 2: InstaPay Integration (Sprint 3-4)
- [ ] Implement `InstaPayClient` with circuit breaker
- [ ] Create `InstantSettlementStrategy` class
- [ ] Add InstaPay configuration and credentials management
- [ ] Implement synchronous risk assessment endpoint

### Phase 3: Wallet Service Updates (Sprint 4-5)
- [ ] Add `InstantSettlementSucceeded` event handler
- [ ] Implement compensation handlers for fund release
- [ ] Add new event types to consumer registry
- [ ] Update ledger service for instant settlement entries

### Phase 4: Risk & Compliance (Sprint 5-6)
- [ ] Create instant settlement rule set
- [ ] Implement velocity checking for instant settlements
- [ ] Add synchronous assessment endpoint
- [ ] Configure stricter thresholds for instant payments

### Phase 5: Testing & Rollout (Sprint 6-7)
- [ ] Integration tests with InstaPay sandbox
- [ ] Chaos testing for failure scenarios
- [ ] Performance testing for latency SLAs
- [ ] Gradual rollout with feature flags

---

## 6. Summary of Key Architectural Decisions

| Decision | Rationale |
|----------|-----------|
| **Separate Strategy** instead of modifying Saga | Preserves async resilience of existing flow; avoids anti-pattern of blocking calls in Saga |
| **Synchronous Risk Check** for instant path | Instant settlements require immediate decision; cannot wait for async background processing |
| **Event-driven Ledger Update** | Preserves event-sourcing integrity; wallet service remains single source of truth for ledger |
| **Compensation via Events** | Ensures auditability; enables retry and idempotency; aligns with existing patterns |
| **Circuit Breaker** for InstaPay | Protects system from external service degradation; enables graceful fallback |
| **Stricter Risk Rules** for instant | Irrevocable nature of instant settlements requires higher scrutiny |

---

## Architectural Proposal: "Instant Settlements" Feature for CrowdPay Connect

**Author:** Lead Architect
